functions.name: Archive.AddContainer
functions.description: Adds a container value to the current archive. Please use Archive.Create before adding the first file and use Archive.Close after the last file to finish. Filename is picked from the container. The plugin does not check for duplicate file names in the archive.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container to compress. We take the file name from container.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name to use. May contain relative path parts. Pass empty to take file name from container.

parameters.name: TimeStamp
parameters.description: The timestamp to use in the archive for the file. Can be empty to use today.

examples.label: Compress PDF containers into a zip file:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
MBS
( "
Folders.UserDesktop
" ); "test.zip" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Archive.Create
"; "zip"; "deflate";
$path
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to create zip archive." ;
$r
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
#
Go to Record/Request/Page [ First ]
Set Variable [
$destPage
; Value: 1 ]
Loop
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Archive.AddContainer";
Merge PDFs::InputPDF
) ]
Go to Record/Request/Page [ Next ; Exit after last: On ]
End Loop
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Archive.Close
") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; MBS( "Folders.UserDesktop" ); "test.zip" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Archive.Create"; "zip"; "deflate"; $path) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to create zip archive." </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result:    </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="16" name="Go to Record/Request/Page">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <RowPageLocation value="First"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$destPage</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Archive.AddContainer"; Merge PDFs::InputPDF) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="16" name="Go to Record/Request/Page">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Exit state="True"/>
    <RowPageLocation value="Next"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Archive.Close") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Archive.AddFile
functions.description: Add files to the current archive. Please use Archive.Create before adding the first file and use Archive.Close after the last file to finish. The plugin does not check for duplicate file names in the archive.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: the list of files and folders to add to archive.

parameters.name: BasePath
parameters.description: The bath path to the folder where to look for files.

parameters.name: TimeStamp
parameters.description: The timestamp to use in the archive for the file. Can be empty to use today.

functions.name: Archive.AddText
functions.description: Add a text file to the archive. Please use Archive.Create before adding the first file and use Archive.Close after the last file to finish. The plugin does not check for duplicate file names in the archive.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to save.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text parameter. Default is native.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name to use. May contain relative path parts.

parameters.name: TimeStamp
parameters.description: The timestamp to use in the archive for the file. Can be empty to use today.

functions.name: Archive.Close
functions.description: Closes the current archive. For file based archives, the file is closed and OK returned. For container based archives, we return the final container value.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename for the zip to use. Only for when we return a container.

functions.result: Returns OK, container value or error.

examples.label: Create a zip as container value and store it:

Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Archive.Create
"; "zip"; "deflate") ]
If [

$r
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]

) ]

;
$zip
]
End If

functions.name: Archive.Compress
functions.signature: MBS( "Archive.Compress"; Format; Filter; Destination; BasePath { ; List; Options; Password } )
functions.description: Compresses files to an archive. Supports various compression algorithms and encryption. The archive usually should not contain the absolute paths of the files, so we need a base path and relative to that base path the list of files and folders to zip.

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: The format to use. Formats: ar, arbsd, argnu, arsvr4, bsdtar, cd9660, cpio, gnutar, iso, iso9660, mtree, mtree-classic, newc, odc, oldtar, pax, paxr, posix, raw, rpax, shar, shardump, ustar, v7tar, v7, warc, xar, zip.

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.description: The filter to use. For zip format can be store, bzip2, zstd or deflate. Filters: b64encode, bzip2, compress, grzip, gzip, lrzip, lz4, lzip, lzma, lzop, uuencode, xz, zstd. Not all filters can be combined with all formats.

parameters.name: Destination
parameters.description: The native file path to the destination archive. Or file name for container.

parameters.name: BasePath
parameters.description: The bath path to the folder where to look for files.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: the list of files and folders to add to archive. If empty, we compress base path as item.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: The options to pass to writer. This is a comma-separated list of options. Option names can be prefixed with module name. Sample options: compression-level=9 zip:encryption=zipcrypt zip:encryption=aes128 zip:encryption=aes256 zip64 gzip:compression-level=9 hdrcharset=UTF-8

parameters.name: Password
parameters.description: The passphrase to use for password protection.

examples.label: Compression examples:
examples.text_code:
# list of files in the folder to archive
Set Variable [
$list
; Value: "minitar.c¶simplebar.rbp¶test/other.txt" ]
# write zip file with password
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Archive.Compress"; "zip"; "deflate"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/minitar.zip"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/";
$list
; "zip:encryption=zipcrypt"; "secret password" ) ]
# Write bzip2 compress tar file:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Archive.Compress"; "ustar"; "bzip2"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/minitar.tar"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/") ]
# and write zip with aes encryption to container file:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Archive.Compress"; "zip"; "deflate"; "minitar.zip"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/";
$list
; "zip:encryption=aes128"; "secret password" ) ]
Set Field [
Archive Read::Container
;
$r
]

examples.label: Compress content of logs folder on server:

$folder
; Value: "/opt/FileMaker/FileMaker Server/Logs" ]
# query list of files
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:
MBS
( "
Files.List
";
$folder
) ]
# where to store the zip file:
Set Variable [
$ZipPath
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
"; "/tmp"; "test.zip") ]
# and now zip these files:
Set Variable [
$archive
; Value:
MBS
( "Archive.Compress"; "zip"; "deflate";
$ZipPath
;
$folder
;
$list
; "compression-level=9¶hdrcharset=UTF-8") ]

examples.label: Compress a file in a let statement:

Let([
filename = "test.jpg";
SourcePath =
MBS
("
Folders.UserDesktop
");
DestPath =
MBS
("
Path.AddPathComponent
";
MBS
("
Folders.UserTemporary
"); "test.zip");
r =
MBS
( "Archive.Compress"; "zip"; "deflate"; DestPath; SourcePath; filename; "zip:encryption=zipcrypt¶hdrcharset=UTF-8"; "12345" )
];r)

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
filename = "test.jpg";
SourcePath = MBS("Folders.UserDesktop");
DestPath = MBS("Path.AddPathComponent"; MBS("Folders.UserTemporary"); "test.zip");
r = MBS( "Archive.Compress"; "zip"; "deflate"; DestPath; SourcePath; filename; "zip:encryption=zipcrypt¶hdrcharset=UTF-8"; "12345" )
];r)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>

functions.name: Archive.CompressContainer
functions.signature: MBS( "Archive.CompressContainer"; Format; Filter; Destination; Container { ; Options; Password } )
functions.description: Compresses one container into a new archive. Supports various compression algorithms and encryption.

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: The format to use. Formats: ar, arbsd, argnu, arsvr4, bsdtar, cd9660, cpio, gnutar, iso, iso9660, mtree, mtree-classic, newc, odc, oldtar, pax, paxr, posix, raw, rpax, shar, shardump, ustar, v7tar, v7, warc, xar, zip.

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.description: The filter to use. For zip format can be store, bzip2, zstd or deflate. Filters: b64encode, bzip2, compress, grzip, gzip, lrzip, lz4, lzip, lzma, lzop, uuencode, xz, zstd. Not all filters can be combined with all formats.

parameters.name: Destination
parameters.description: The native file path to the destination archive. Or file name for container.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container to compress. We take the file name from container.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: The options to pass to writer. This is a comma-separated list of options. Option names can be prefixed with module name. Sample options: compression-level=9 zip:encryption=zipcrypt zip:encryption=aes128 zip:encryption=aes256 zip64 gzip:compression-level=9 hdrcharset=UTF-8

parameters.name: Password
parameters.description: The passphrase to use for password protection.

examples.label: Compress a container:
examples.text_code:
Set Field [
MyFile::CompressedFile
;
MBS
( "Archive.CompressContainer"; "zip"; "deflate"; "";
MyFile::SomeContainer
; "hdrcharset=UTF-8" ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "Archive.CompressContainer"; "zip"; "deflate"; ""; MyFile::SomeContainer; "hdrcharset=UTF-8" ) </Calculation>
    <Field table="MyFile" id="99999" name="CompressedFile "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Compress PDF and add to email:
examples.text_code:
# get some PDF from a field
Set Variable [
$PDF
; Value:
Reports::ReportContainer
]
# Compress it
Set Variable [
$CompressedPDF
; Value:
MBS
( "Archive.CompressContainer"; "zip"; "deflate"; "report.zip";
$pdf
) ]
# now add to email
Set Variable [
$email
; Value:
MBS
( "
SendMail.CreateEmail
") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SendMail.AddAttachmentContainer
";
$Email
;
$CompressedPDF
; "report.zip"; "application/zip" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SendMail.SetPlainText
";
$email
; "Dear " &
Contacts::FullName
& ¶ & ¶ & "Attached you find the report from " & Get(CurrentDate ) & ¶ & ¶ & "Greetings" & ¶ & "Your FileMaker Server") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get some PDF from a field</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Reports::ReportContainer </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$PDF</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Compress it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Archive.CompressContainer"; "zip"; "deflate"; "report.zip"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$CompressedPDF</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now add to email</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SendMail.CreateEmail") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$email</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SendMail.AddAttachmentContainer"; $Email; $CompressedPDF; "report.zip"; "application/zip" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SendMail.SetPlainText"; $email; "Dear " & Contacts::FullName & ¶ & ¶ & "Attached you find the report from " & Get(CurrentDate ) & ¶ & ¶ & "Greetings" & ¶ & "Your FileMaker Server") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Archive.CompressText
functions.signature: MBS( "Archive.CompressText"; Format; Filter; Destination; FileName; Text; Encoding { ; Options; Password } )
functions.description: Compresses a text into a new archive. Supports various compression algorithms and encryption.

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: The format to use. Formats: ar, arbsd, argnu, arsvr4, bsdtar, cd9660, cpio, gnutar, iso, iso9660, mtree, mtree-classic, newc, odc, oldtar, pax, paxr, posix, raw, rpax, shar, shardump, ustar, v7tar, v7, warc, xar, zip.

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.description: The filter to use. For zip format can be store, bzip2, zstd or deflate. Filters: b64encode, bzip2, compress, grzip, gzip, lrzip, lz4, lzip, lzma, lzop, uuencode, xz, zstd. Not all filters can be combined with all formats.

parameters.name: Destination
parameters.description: The native file path to the destination archive. Or file name for container.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name or path to use for the entry in the archive.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to compress. We use UTF-8 encoding.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text parameter. Default is UTF-8.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: The options to pass to writer. This is a comma-separated list of options. Option names can be prefixed with module name. Sample options: compression-level=9 zip:encryption=zipcrypt zip:encryption=aes128 zip:encryption=aes256 zip64 gzip:compression-level=9 hdrcharset=UTF-8

parameters.name: Password
parameters.description: The passphrase to use for password protection.

; Value:
MBS

    </Value>

    </Repetition>

functions.name: Archive.Content
functions.description: Lists content of archive as JSON. JSON has an entry count for the file count, an entry format for the format type and an entry for files array.

parameters.name: Archive
parameters.description: Either container with archive or native file path.

parameters.name: Passphrase
parameters.description: Password to use.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: The options to pass to reader. This is a comma-separated list of options. Option names can be prefixed with module name.

examples.label: Check archive content:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:
MBS
("Archive.Content"; If(IsEmpty(
$Path
);
$Container
;
$Path
)) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to read container" ;
$list
]
Else
Set Variable [
$Content
; Value:
MBS
("
JSON.Colorize
";
$list
) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Archive.Content"; If(IsEmpty($Path); $Container; $Path)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to read container" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$list </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.Colorize"; $list) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Content</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Archive.Create
functions.signature: MBS( "Archive.Create"; Format; Filter { ; Destination; Options; Password } )
functions.description: Creates a new archive. You may call Archive.AddContainer , Archive.AddFile and Archive.AddText to add files and later call Archive.Close function to finish. Supports various compression algorithms and encryption. The archive usually should not contain the absolute paths of the files, so we need a base path and relative to that base path the list of files and folders to zip.

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: The format to use. Formats: ar, arbsd, argnu, arsvr4, bsdtar, cd9660, cpio, gnutar, iso, iso9660, mtree, mtree-classic, newc, odc, oldtar, pax, paxr, posix, raw, rpax, shar, shardump, ustar, v7tar, v7, warc, xar, zip.

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.description: The filter to use. For zip format can be store, bzip2, zstd or deflate. Filters: b64encode, bzip2, compress, grzip, gzip, lrzip, lz4, lzip, lzma, lzop, uuencode, xz, zstd. Not all filters can be combined with all formats.

parameters.name: Destination
parameters.description: The native file path to the destination archive. Or empty or file name for container.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: The options to pass to writer. This is a comma-separated list of options. Option names can be prefixed with module name. Sample options: compression-level=9 zip:encryption=zipcrypt zip:encryption=aes128 zip:encryption=aes256 zip64 gzip:compression-level=9 hdrcharset=UTF-8

parameters.name: Password
parameters.description: The passphrase to use for password protection. Usually only used when an encryption option is set.

functions.name: Archive.Extract
functions.signature: MBS( "Archive.Extract"; Archive; FileList; DestinationFolder { ; Passphrase; Options } )
functions.description: Extracts files in an archive. Restores file permissions, times, Mac metadata and file flags. Use Archive.ExtractFile if you only need one file as container. You may need to use Plugin.LoadIconvLibrary to load iconv library for text conversion.

parameters.name: Archive
parameters.description: Either container with archive or native file path.

parameters.name: FileList
parameters.description: Optional list of files to extract. Pass empty text when you need all files.

parameters.name: DestinationFolder
parameters.description: The native path to the destination folder.

parameters.name: Passphrase
parameters.description: Password to use.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: The options to pass to reader. This is a comma-separated list of options. Option names can be prefixed with module name.

examples.label: Expand an archive:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Archive.Extract"; 
"/Users/cs/Desktop/invoices-2019-02-12_1924.zip"; /* path to archive (or container) */
"" /* all files */; 
"/Users/cs/Desktop/"; /* dest */ 
"98234kjh245234" /* password */)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Archive.ExtractFile
functions.signature: MBS( "Archive.ExtractFile"; Archive; FilePath { ; Passphrase; New file name; Options } )
functions.description: Extracts one file and returns as container. You may use Archive.FileList to get a list of file names in the archive. For pure gz file without an archive, you can use Archive.GZipDecompress function.

parameters.name: Archive
parameters.description: Either container with archive or native file path.

parameters.name: FilePath
parameters.description: The path name of the file within the archive. Or since version 14.3 the index of the item starting with 0 for the first file.

parameters.name: Passphrase
parameters.description: Password to use for the archive.

parameters.name: New file name
parameters.description: The new file name to use for container. Default is to use name of the item in the zip archive.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: The options to pass to reader. This is a comma-separated list of options. Option names can be prefixed with module name.

functions.name: Archive.FileList
functions.description: Lists file paths in the archive. Please use Archive.Content if you need more details. This provides file list, which you can filter and then pass to Archive.Extract .

parameters.name: Archive
parameters.description: Either container with archive or native file path.

parameters.name: Passphrase
parameters.description: Password to use.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: The options to pass to reader. This is a comma-separated list of options. Option names can be prefixed with module name.

examples.label: Get file list:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:
MBS
("Archive.FileList"; If(IsEmpty(
$Path
);
$Container
;
$Path
)) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to read container" ;
$list
]
Else
Set Variable [
$Content
; Value:
MBS
("
JSON.Colorize
";
$list
) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Archive.FileList"; If(IsEmpty($Path); $Container; $Path)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to read container" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$list </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.Colorize"; $list) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Content</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Archive.GZipDecompress
functions.signature: MBS( "Archive.GZipDecompress"; Archive { ; New file name; Options } )
functions.description: Extracts content of gzip file as container. You may use Archive.FileList to get a list of file names in the archive. Since a pure gz file doesn't have an archive inside like a tar.gz, this function will return the uncompressed content as new file. Please pass file name to have FileMaker handle the file type correctly, e.g. show picture.

parameters.name: Archive
parameters.description: Either container with archive or native file path.

parameters.name: New file name
parameters.description: The new file name to use for container. Default is "data".

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: The options to pass to reader. This is a comma-separated list of options. Option names can be prefixed with module name.

examples.label: Extract gzip file:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Archive.GZipDecompress"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.jpg.gz"; "test.jpg") ]
Set Field [
Contacts::Photo
Container ;
$r
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Archive.GZipDecompress"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.jpg.gz"; "test.jpg") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    <Field table="Contacts" id="99999" name="Photo Container "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Extract text from gzip compressed text with prefixed length:
examples.text_code:
# get some response string, gzip compressed with length before
// Set Variable [
$response
; Value:
GzipDecompress::InputString
]
Set Variable [
$response
; Value: "RAAAAB+LCAAAAAAABACrZlBicGbIZ0hhSAWyrBhMGAwYDBl0gOwwhkSGHIZSqLgSEIJEg4BqSxlKkE RrGQAjtv6kRAAAAA==" ]
# convert base64 to Hex
Set Variable [
$hex
; Value:
MBS
( "
Text.DecodeHexFromBase64
";
$response
) ]
# now we can remove the first 4 bytes with length
Set Variable [
$hexNum
; Value: Left(
$hex
; 8) ]
Set Variable [
$Rest
; Value: Middle (
$hex
; 9; 100000000 ) ]
# we can decode the length value
Set Variable [
$len
; Value:
MBS
("
Math.DecodeNumber
"; "Integer"; 32;
$hexNum
) ]
# and read to container value from hex
Set Variable [
$Cont
; Value:
MBS
( "
Container.DecodeFromHex
"; "FILE";
$Rest
; "test.gz" ) ]
# now decompress gzip
Set Variable [
$Decompressed
; Value:
MBS
( "Archive.GZipDecompress";
$cont
; "test.txt") ]
# and read it as text. it is little endian UTF-16 here!
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
Text.ReadTextFromContainer
";
$Decompressed
; "UTF-16LE") ]
# show in field
Set Field [
GzipDecompress::OutputString
;
$result
]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Result" ;
$result
]
Example result:
{
"Code"
:
401
,
"Value"
:
""
,
"Route"
:
""
}
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get some response string, gzip compressed with length before</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="False" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GzipDecompress::InputString </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$response</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"RAAAAB+LCAAAAAAABACrZlBicGbIZ0hhSAWyrBhMGAwYDBl0gOwwhkSGHIZSqLgSEIJEg4BqSxlKkE RrGQAjtv6kRAAAAA==" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$response</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> convert base64 to Hex</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.DecodeHexFromBase64"; $response ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$hex</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now we can remove the first 4 bytes with length</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Left($hex; 8) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$hexNum</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Middle ( $hex; 9; 100000000 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Rest</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> we can decode the length value</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Math.DecodeNumber"; "Integer"; 32; $hexNum) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$len</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and read to container value from hex</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.DecodeFromHex"; "FILE"; $Rest; "test.gz" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Cont</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now decompress gzip</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Archive.GZipDecompress"; $cont; "test.txt") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Decompressed</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and read it as text. it is little endian UTF-16 here!</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.ReadTextFromContainer"; $Decompressed; "UTF-16LE") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> show in field</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$result </Calculation>
    <Field table="GzipDecompress" id="99999" name="OutputString "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Result" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$result </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Archive.IsOpen
functions.description: Queries whether an archive is currently open. Returns 1 if we have an open archive, e.g. if you called Archive.Create before, but you haven't yet called Archive.Close .

examples.label: Check the function:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r1
; Value:
MBS
( "Archive.IsOpen" ) ]
#  create in-memory archive
Set Variable [
$ro
; Value:
MBS
( "
Archive.Create
"; "zip"; "deflate"; "test.zip" ) ]
#  check again -> now true
Set Variable [
$r2
; Value:
MBS
( "Archive.IsOpen" ) ]
#  close archive
Set Variable [
$rc
; Value:
MBS
( "
Archive.Close
" ) ]
#  check again -> now false
Set Variable [
$r3
; Value:
MBS
( "Archive.IsOpen" ) ]
# shows 010, so the function works.
Show Custom Dialog [ "Results" ;
$r1
&
$r2
&
$r3
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Archive.IsOpen" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>  create in-memory archive</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Archive.Create"; "zip"; "deflate"; "test.zip" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ro</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>  check again -> now true</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Archive.IsOpen" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>  close archive</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Archive.Close" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$rc</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>  check again -> now false</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Archive.IsOpen" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r3</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> shows 010, so the function works.</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Results" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r1 & $r2 & $r3 </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CNGroup.Name
functions.description: Queries group name.

parameters.name: GroupIdentifier
parameters.description: The group identifier. Can be identifier or group name.

examples.label: Queries group names:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>While( 
[ 
	names = ""; 
	// query list of group IDs
	list = MBS( "CNContactStore.Groups" ); 
	count = ValueCount(list); 
	index = 1
] ; 
	index ≤ count ; 
[ 
	id = GetValue(list; index); 
	// query name for each ID
	name = MBS( "CNGroup.Name"; id); 
	names = names & id & " -> " & name & ¶; 
	index = index + 1 
] ; 
// return all the names
names )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: The window reference for the showing menu relative to a window.

parameters.name: Item

parameters.name: objectName
parameters.description: The object name for mode=object.

functions.name: ContinuityCamera.SetScriptTrigger
functions.description: Sets the script to trigger. When some picture or document is available, we call the script. Parameter to the script is currently the document type, e.g. JPEG, TIFF or PDF. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

examples.label: Set trigger:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("ContinuityCamera.SetScriptTrigger"; Get(FileName); "ContinuityCameraTrigger") ]

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

The context size represents the total number of tokens that can be used in a single session, including both input prompts and generated responses. Returns an error if the context size cannot be determined. Typically this is due to the model not being available or Apple Intelligence is disabled.

functions.name: FoundationModels.GetMaximumResponseTokens
functions.description: Queries the maximum number of tokens the model is allowed to produce in its response. If the model produce `maximumResponseTokens` before it naturally completes its response, the response will be terminated early. No error will be thrown. This property can be used to protect against unexpectedly verbose responses and runaway generations. If no value is specified, then the model is allowed to produce the longest answer its context size supports. If the response exceeds that limit without terminating, an error will be thrown. Returns empty value if not set.

parameters.name: FoundationModel
parameters.description: The identifier for the model.

examples.label: Queries maximum response tokens:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$MaximumResponseTokens
; Value:
MBS
( "FoundationModels.GetMaximumResponseTokens" ) ]

    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$MaximumResponseTokens</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FoundationModels.List
functions.description: Returns the list of model identifiers.

functions.name: FoundationModels.Prewarm
functions.description: Prewarm the model. Requests that the system eagerly load the resources required for this session into memory and optionally caches a prefix of your prompt. This method can be useful in cases where you have a strong signal that the user will interact with  session within a few seconds. For example, you might call prewarm when the user begins typing into a text field. If you know a prefix for the future prompt, passing it to prewarm will allow the system to process the prompt eagerly and reduce latency for the future request. Important: You should only use prewarm when you have a window of at least 1s before the call to respond. Note: Calling this method does not guarantee that the system loads your assets immediately, particularly if your app is running in the background or the system is under load.

parameters.name: FoundationModel
parameters.description: The identifier for the model.

parameters.name: Prefix
parameters.description: The prefix text.

functions.name: FoundationModels.Release
functions.description: Release memory for a loaded model.

parameters.name: FoundationModel
parameters.description: The identifier for the model.

functions.name: FoundationModels.ReleaseAll
functions.description: Releases all models.

functions.name: FoundationModels.Respond
functions.description: Produces a response to a prompt. We wait for the answer and return it. If temperature >= 0 or maximumResponseTokens >= 0, we pass these as options.

parameters.name: FoundationModel
parameters.description: The identifier for the model.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to send to the LLM.

examples.label: Summarize text:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$session
; Value:
MBS
("
FoundationModels.Initialize
"; "General"; "PermissiveContentTransformations") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
FoundationModels.BeginSession
";
$session
; "Summarize the following text in 100 words.") ]
#
# now ask the model for an answer, which may take a few seconds
Set Variable [
$answer
; Value:
MBS
("FoundationModels.Respond";
$session
;
Summarize::Text
to Summerize) ]
#
# store answer
Set Field [
Summarize::Summary
;
$answer
]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
#
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
FoundationModels.Release
";
$session
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("FoundationModels.Initialize"; "General"; "PermissiveContentTransformations") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$session</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("FoundationModels.BeginSession"; $session; "Summarize the following text in 100 words.") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now ask the model for an answer, which may take a few seconds</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("FoundationModels.Respond"; $session; Summarize::Text to Summerize) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$answer</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> store answer</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$answer </Calculation>
    <Field table="Summarize" id="99999" name="Summary "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>

functions.name: FoundationModels.SetMaximumResponseTokens
functions.description: Sets the maximum number of tokens the model is allowed to produce in its response. If the model produce `maximumResponseTokens` before it naturally completes its response, the response will be terminated early. No error will be thrown. This property can be used to protect against unexpectedly verbose responses and runaway generations. If no value is specified, then the model is allowed to produce the longest answer its context size supports. If the response exceeds that limit without terminating, an error will be thrown.

parameters.name: FoundationModel
parameters.description: The identifier for the model.

parameters.name: maximumResponseTokens
parameters.description: The maximum response tokens to use.

examples.label: Set maximum response tokens to 100:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "FoundationModels.SetMaximumResponseTokens"; 100 ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FoundationModels.SetMaximumResponseTokens"; 100 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FoundationModels.SetTemperature
functions.description: Sets the temperature. The temperature influences the confidence of the models response. The value of this property must be a number between `0` and `1` inclusive. Temperature is an adjustment applied to the probability distribution prior to sampling. A value of `1` results in no adjustment. Values less than `1` will make the probability distribution sharper, with already likely tokens becoming even more likely. The net effect is that low temperatures manifest as more stable and predictable responses, while high temperatures give the model more creative license. Note: Leaving `temperature` unset lets the system choose a reasonable default on your behalf.

parameters.name: FoundationModel
parameters.description: The identifier for the model.

parameters.name: temperature
parameters.description: The temperature.

examples.label: Set temperature:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "FoundationModels.SetTemperature";
1.0
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FoundationModels.SetTemperature"; 1.0 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: GZipFile.Compress
functions.description: Compresses a file. This function uses gzip file format, so please use file extension .gz. Maximum compression is used. As this function processes file in chunks it should work fine with huge files. In case of an error like a full hard disk, you need to manually delete the half written output file. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: InputPath
parameters.description: The native path to the input file.

parameters.name: OutputPath
parameters.description: The native path to the input file.

functions.name: JS.AddFileMakerRunScriptFunction
functions.description: Adds a function to start scripts from JavaScript. Scripts are queued and run later when JavaScript finished. The function in JavaScript returns the error code from FileMaker, which is zero for success. For increased security we prefer all FileMaker developers define their own function name, so if you get some foreign JavaScript, it can't know the function name and mess with FileMaker. Be aware that this function may launch any script in the solution, even those not shown in the menu. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: JSRef
parameters.description: The JavaScript reference number.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the function.

";
$JS

Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
JS.Release
";
$JS
) ]

	Set Variable [ $$js ; Value: MBS( "JS.New" ) ] 
	Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS( "JS.AddFunction"; $$js; "makeCRCTable"; "function makeCRCTable(){
        var c;
        var crcTable = [];
        for(var n =0; n < 256; n++){
                c = n;
                for(var k =0; k < 8; k++){
                        c = ((c&1) ? (0xEDB88320 ^ (c >>> 1)) : (c >>> 1));
                }
                crcTable[n] = c;
        }
        return crcTable;
}") ] 
	Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS( "JS.AddFunction"; $$js; "crc32"; "function crc32(str) {
        var crcTable = crcTable || (crcTable = makeCRCTable());
        var crc = 0 ^ (-1);

        for (var i = 0; i < str.length; i++ ) {
                crc = (crc >>> 8) ^ crcTable[(crc ^ str.charCodeAt(i)) & 0xFF];
        }

        return (crc ^ (-1)) >>> 0;
}") ] 
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JS.CF
functions.description: Calls a global function given the name with FileMaker values. Please initialize the global "js" JavaScript environment before (see JS.New ) and fill it with function definitions as needed (see JS.AddFunction ). This may include functions to do FileMaker evaluate (see JS.AddFileMakerEvaluateFunction ), SQL execute (see JS.AddFileMakerSQLFunction ) and starting scripts (see JS.AddFileMakerRunScriptFunction ). Parameters are passed to the function. Please pass as many parameters in FileMaker as needed. Numbers are passed as number and other values are passed as text. If the function needs more parameter, undefined values are used to fill up. If the function needs less parameters, additional ones are dropped. There is no limit in the number of parameters for MBS Plugin here. Returns the result of the function converted to FileMaker, e.g. as text or number value. Same as JS.CallFunctionValues , but takes "js" as the global JavaScript engine. See our example for JavaScript Custom Functions. See also FM. CF and CF function to do custom functions with FileMaker expressions. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Parameters parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the function.

parameters.name: Parameters...
parameters.description: Value to pass as parameter.

functions.name: JS.CallFunctionValues
functions.description: Calls a global function given the name with FileMaker values. Parameters are passed to the function. Please pass as many parameters in FileMaker as needed. Numbers are passed as number and other values are passed as text. If the function needs more parameter, undefined values are used to fill up. If the function needs less parameters, additional ones are dropped. Returns the result of the function converted to FileMaker, e.g. as text or number value. If you like to use JSON instead, please use JS.CallFunction . Or pass JSON as string and let JavaScript decode it as part of the function. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Parameters parameter as often as you need.

functions.name: JS.Evaluate
functions.description: Evaluates a JavaScript expression. This may include definition of functions and objects and using them. Result is converted to JSON and returned. The evaluate runs within the FileMaker process. If you cause an endless loop, FileMaker freezes and needs to be force quit, so please always include an exit condition for your loops. Also if you get JavaScript to crash, FileMaker will crash with it.

parameters.name: JSRef
parameters.description: The JavaScript reference number.

parameters.name: JavaScript
parameters.description: A JavaScript expression.

examples.label: Defines and runs a function in JavaScript:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
f = "
function makeCRCTable(){
    var c;
    var crcTable = [];
    for(var n =0; n < 256; n++){
        c = n;
        for(var k =0; k < 8; k++){
            c = ((c&1) ? (0xEDB88320 ^ (c >>> 1)) : (c >>> 1));
        }
        crcTable[n] = c;
    }
    return crcTable;
}

function crc32(str) {
    var crcTable = crcTable || (crcTable = makeCRCTable());
    var crc = 0 ^ (-1);

    for (var i = 0; i < str.length; i++ ) {
        crc = (crc >>> 8) ^ crcTable[(crc ^ str.charCodeAt(i)) & 0xFF];
    }

    return (crc ^ (-1)) >>> 0;
};

crc32(InputValue);";
j = MBS( "JS.New" );
r = MBS( "JS.SetGlobalPropertyValue"; j; "InputValue"; "Hello World" );
r = MBS( "JS.Evaluate"; j; f );
x = MBS( "JS.Release"; j )
];r)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

function crc32(str) {

examples.label: Defines and runs a function in JavaScript:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
f = "
function makeCRCTable(){
    var c;
    var crcTable = [];
    for(var n =0; n < 256; n++){
        c = n;
        for(var k =0; k < 8; k++){
            c = ((c&1) ? (0xEDB88320 ^ (c >>> 1)) : (c >>> 1));
        }
        crcTable[n] = c;
    }
    return crcTable;
}

function crc32(str) {
    var crcTable = crcTable || (crcTable = makeCRCTable());
    var crc = 0 ^ (-1);

    for (var i = 0; i < str.length; i++ ) {
        crc = (crc >>> 8) ^ crcTable[(crc ^ str.charCodeAt(i)) & 0xFF];
    }

    return (crc ^ (-1)) >>> 0;
};

crc32(InputValue);";
j = MBS( "JS.New" );
r = MBS( "JS.SetGlobalPropertyValue"; j; "InputValue"; "Hello World" );
r = MBS( "JS.EvaluateToString"; j; f );
x = MBS( "JS.Release"; j )
];r)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JS.InitModules
functions.description: Installs Duktape module loading. Only calls JS.InitModules once on a new instance of the JavaScript engine. Define your own Duktape.modSearch() to provide environment specific module lookups. After these steps, require() will be registered to the global object and the module system is ready to use.

parameters.name: JSRef
parameters.description: The JavaScript reference number.

examples.label: Create JavaScript environment and enable modules:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$$JS
; Value:
MBS
("
JS.New
") ]

Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "JS.InitModules";
$$js
) ]

functions.name: JS.New
functions.description: Creates a new JavaScript engine instance. This object contains all the functions and properties you defined. Please release the object later with the JS.Release function. Your own JavaScript engine is useful for: Running some snippet of JavaScript code without web viewer. Using various JavaScript libraries Providing a scripting language to your own solution. Doing JavaScript on server side. Working with JSON data Enjoy using regular expressions, unicode text functions, number parsing and formatting with no platform dependencies If JSRef is "js", we use this one as the global JavaScript engine for custom functions and JS. CF function. See our example JavaScript Custom Functions. When using a name for the reference number, you can use this reference anywhere you need to pass a JavaScript reference number. Check also our JavaScriptWebKit functions for using WebKit’s JavaScript engine on macOS and iOS.

parameters.name: JSRef
parameters.description: The desired js reference. Usually each one gets a number assigned, but you can create a JavaScript environment with a fixed name, so you can anywhere reference it by name.

functions.result: Returns JS reference number or error.

functions.name: JS.Now
functions.description: Queries current time as double value. This is seconds since 1st January 1970 multiplied by 1000 and may contain fraction with microseconds. Get current time in POSIX milliseconds, as seen by the ECMAScript environment. The return value matches Date.now() with the reservation that sub-millisecond resolution may be available.

</fmxmlsnippet>

      <Calculation>$v1 & ¶ & $v2 & ¶ & $v3 </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JS.Release"; $JS ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JS.SetGlobalPropertyValue
functions.description: Sets a global property. Please pass value as number or text. Container, date, time and timestamp are converted to text. This is a simplified version of JS.SetGlobalProperty to avoid extra JSON encoding for simple values.

parameters.name: JSRef
parameters.description: The JavaScript reference number.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the variable.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The number or text value.

examples.label: Sets a variable with name:

MBS
( "JS.SetGlobalPropertyValue";
$js
; "Hello"; 123,45 )
examples.xml_code:

    <Value>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: JS.StringCount

parameters.name: JSRef
parameters.description: The JavaScript reference number.

Set Variable [
$JS
; Value:
MBS
( "
JS.New
" ) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "JavaScript memory statistics:" ;

( "JS.StringCount";
$JS
) & " strings" & ¶ &
MBS
( "
JS.ObjectCount
";
$JS
) & " objects" ]
examples.xml_code:

    </Repetition>

functions.name: JS.TimeToDateComponents
functions.description: Converts time to date components. Convert a time value to components (year, month, day, hour, minute, second) interpreted in UTC. If the time value is invalid, e.g. beyond the valid ECMAScript time range, an error is thrown.

parameters.name: JSRef
parameters.description: The JavaScript reference number.

parameters.name: Time
parameters.description: The time value.

examples.label: Query date components for now:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "JS.TimeToDateComponents";
$$JS
;
MBS
( "
JS.Now
";
$$JS
))
Example result:
{
"year"
:
2020
,
"month"
:
1
,
"day"
:
19
,
"hours"
:
12
,
"minutes"
:
59
,
"seconds"
:
13
,
"milliseconds"
:
806
,
"weekday"
:
0
,
"timezone"
:
"UTC"
,
"weekdayText"
:
"Sunday"
}
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JS.TimeToDateComponents"; $$JS; MBS( "JS.Now"; $$JS ))</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Query time in JavaScript, convert to UTC and format in FileMaker:

[
~js =

JS.Now
"; ~js ));
~releasejs =

functions.name: JS.UTCDateToTime
functions.description: Converts date to time value. Convert components (year, month, day, hour, minute, seconds) in timestamp, interpreted in UTC, into a time value.

parameters.name: JSRef
parameters.description: The JavaScript reference number.

parameters.name: TS
parameters.description: The timestamp to convert. Must be in UTC timezone.

examples.label: Tests time functions:
examples.text_code:
Let([
c =
MBS
( "
JS.New
" );
r =
MBS
( "JS.UTCDateToTime"; c; Get(CurrentTimeStamp) );
d =
MBS
( "
JS.TimeToDateComponents
"; c; r )
]; r & ¶ & d)
Example result:
1582121231000
{
"year":   2020,
"month":   2,
"day":   19,
"hours":   14,
"minutes":   7,
"seconds":   11,
"milliseconds":   0,
"weekday":   3,
"timezone":   "UTC",
"weekdayText":   "Wednesday"
}
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
c = MBS( "JS.New" );
r = MBS( "JS.UTCDateToTime"; c; Get(CurrentTimeStamp) );
d = MBS( "JS.TimeToDateComponents"; c; r )
]; r & ¶ & d)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MapView.AddPoint

examples.label: Create MBS Pin on map with custom image:

$json
; Value: "{  \"address\": \"Nickenich\",  \"title\": \"Monkeybread Software\",  \"subtitle\": \"
MBS
is here\",  \"imageWidth\": 40,  \"imageHeight\": 40 }" ]
# add image from container field to JSON
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.AddStringToObject
";
$json
; "image"; Base64Encode (
MapView::image
) ) ]
# show
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MapView.AddPoint";
$$MapView
;
$JSON
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

examples.label: Create circle of pins around current region:
examples.text_code:
# create some pins on a circle around a given point
Set Variable [
$centerLat
; Value:
MBS
( "
MapView.GetCenterCoordinateLatitude
";
$$MapView
) ]
Set Variable [
$centerLon
; Value:
MBS
( "
MapView.GetCenterCoordinateLongitude
";
$$MapView
) ]
#
# loop counting up from 1 to
$count
Set Variable [
$count
; Value: 12 ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 1 ]
If [
$index
≤
$count
]
Loop
# your script steps here
Set Variable [
$angle
; Value: 360 * (
$index
/ 12) ]
Set Variable [
$a
; Value:
$angle
/ 180 * Pi ]
#
# calculate new coordinates
Set Variable [
$fLat
; Value: ,005 ]
Set Variable [
$fLon
; Value: ,01 ]
Set Variable [
$lat
; Value:
$centerLat
+ Sin(
$a
) *
$fLat
]
Set Variable [
$lon
; Value:
$centerLon
+ Cos(
$a
) *
$fLon
]
#
Set Variable [
$json
; Value: "{}" ]
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.AddNumberToObject
";
$json
; "latitude";
$lat
) ]
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.AddNumberToObject
";
$json
; "longitude";
$lon
) ]
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.AddNumberToObject
";
$json
; "clusteringIdentifier"; "pin") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MapView.AddPoint";
$$MapView
;
$JSON
) ]
#
# next
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$index
>
$count
]
End Loop
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> create some pins on a circle around a given point</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MapView.GetCenterCoordinateLatitude"; $$MapView ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$centerLat</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MapView.GetCenterCoordinateLongitude"; $$MapView ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$centerLon</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> loop counting up from 1 to $count</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>12 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $index ≤ $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> your script steps here</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>360 * ($index / 12) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$angle</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$angle / 180 * Pi </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$a</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> calculate new coordinates</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>,005 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$fLat</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>,01 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$fLon</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$centerLat + Sin($a) * $fLat </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$lat</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$centerLon + Cos($a) * $fLon </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$lon</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"{}" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.AddNumberToObject"; $json; "latitude"; $lat ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.AddNumberToObject"; $json; "longitude"; $lon ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.AddNumberToObject"; $json; "clusteringIdentifier"; "pin") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MapView.AddPoint"; $$MapView; $JSON ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index > $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

      <Calculation>MBS( "MapView.AddPolyline"; $$MapView; "{  \"coordinates\": [{\"address\": \"Hamburg, Germany\"}, {\"address\": \"Munich, Germany\"}, {\"address\": \"Cologne, Germany\"}],  \"strokeColor\": \"#FF000077\" }") </Calculation>

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MapView.CreateWithControl
functions.description: Creates a new map view on the given window covering the area of the given control. Use MapView.Release later to destroy the image view. If you use a register (tab controls) on the window, be aware that the image view actually is in front of the register (tab controls). So you need to check in the script which register page is current and should show what image view. Or by a trigger you turn the visibility on and off. Image view reference numbers are starting at 78000 and counting up for each new map view. Not supported for MacOS 32-bit. Does not work for Web Direct. When creating on a card window, please pass reference to parent window or just 0 for front window. On MacOS you may need to pass delta y value to adjust position. If you create a control on a new window, you may do window update command in your script first to have FileMaker actually build the window with controls and display it. A short script pause or using FM.RunScriptLater with 0.1 delay can help having all controls draw themselves. Then you can use this command to put our control on top. We use GetLayoutObjectAttribute() to query left, top, width and height and this gives empty value if control is not yet here. Creation fails to pick the right window in case the window is selected by name and the window name is not unique. In that case better work with window reference numbers or just pass 0 for current front window.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: ControlName
parameters.description: The name of the control on your FileMaker form. e.g. you can place a rectangle there to define the position.

parameters.name: DX
parameters.description: A delta to move drop area more to left or right.

parameters.name: DY
parameters.description: A delta to move drop area more to top or bottom.

functions.result: Returns map view reference number or error.

functions.name: MapView.CreateWithSize
functions.signature: MBS( "MapView.CreateWithSize"; WindowRef; X; Y; W; H )
functions.description: Creates a new map view inside the window with the given rectangle. Use MapView.Release later to destroy the image view. If you use a register (tab controls) on the window, be aware that the image view actually is in front of the register (tab controls). So you need to check in the script which register page is current and should show what image view. Or by a trigger you turn the visibility on and off. Image view reference numbers are starting at 78000 and counting up for each new map view. Not supported for MacOS 32-bit. Does not work for Web Direct.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: The left coordinate in the window.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: The top coordinate in the window.

parameters.name: W
parameters.description: The width of the image view in the window.

parameters.name: H
parameters.description: The height of the image view in the window.

functions.result: Returns map view reference number or error.

functions.name: MapView.IsUserLocationVisible
functions.description: Whether the device’s current location is visible in the map view. This property tells you whether the icon used to represent the user’s current location is visible in the map view. When determining whether the current location is visible, this property factors in the horizontal accuracy of the location data. Specifically, if the rectangle represented by the user’s current location plus or minus minus the horizontal accuracy of that location intersects the map’s visible rectangle, this property contains the value 1. If that location rectangle does not intersect the map’s visible rectangle, this property contains the value 0. If the user’s location cannot be determined, this property contains the value 0.

parameters.name: MapView
parameters.description: The map view reference number.

parameters.description: The address where to end.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: Various options. Add 1 to show route on map. Add 2 to show alternative routes on the map. Add 4 to show start of route with a pin. Add 8 to show end of route with a pin. Add 16 to zoom map to show whole of the router. Add 32 to return result as JSON. Add 64 to include poly lines in JSON.

parameters.name: TransportType
parameters.description: The type of transportation to use. Value can be a combination of 1 for car, 2 for walk. Default is by car.

parameters.name: StartIdentifier
parameters.description: The identifier for the pin to use with script trigger. If empty, the plugin assigns a number automatically.

parameters.name: EndIdentifier
parameters.description: The identifier for the pin to use with script trigger. If empty, the plugin assigns a number automatically.

parameters.name: StartColor
parameters.description: The color to use. Can be a color name or hex notation like in HTML.

parameters.name: EndColor
parameters.description: The color to use. Can be a color name or hex notation like in HTML.

examples.label: Plan a route and store JSON:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("MapView.PlanRoute";
$$MapView
; "Köln"; "München"; 1+2+4+8+16+32; 1+2+4) ]
Set Field [
MapView::Route
;
$r
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("MapView.PlanRoute"; $$MapView; "Köln"; "München"; 1+2+4+8+16+32; 1+2+4) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    <Field table="MapView" id="99999" name="Route "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Plan route with coordinates:

("MapView.PlanRoute";
$$MapView
;
"50.938056N 6.956944E"; // Cologne, Germany
"48.137222N 11.575556E"; // Munich, Germany
1+4+8+16; 1)
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

"50.938056N 6.956944E"; // Cologne, Germany
"48.137222N 11.575556E"; // Munich, Germany
1+4+8+16; 1)</Calculation>

</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Save screenshot to desktop:
examples.text_code:
If [ Length (
$$MapView
) > 0 ]
Set Variable [
$screenshot
; Value:
MBS
("MapView.Screenshot";
$$MapView
; "PNG") ]
Set Variable [
$file
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
MBS
( "
Folders.UserDesktop
" ); "mapview.png" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Container.WriteFile
";
$screenshot
;
$file
) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ Length ( $$MapView) > 0 ] 
	Set Variable [ $screenshot ; Value: MBS("MapView.Screenshot"; $$MapView; "PNG") ] 
	Set Variable [ $file ; Value: MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; MBS( "Folders.UserDesktop" ); "mapview.png" ) ] 
	Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS( "Container.WriteFile"; $screenshot; $file) ] 
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MapView.SetRegion
functions.description: Sets the current region.

parameters.name: MapView
parameters.description: The map view reference number.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: An JSON with region details. Can be degree or meter based for span. latitude and longitude to define center coordinate. Add latitudeMeters/longitudeMeters or latitudeDelta/longitudeDelta to define span. If there is an address key, we query coordiantes of the given address string instead of using coordinates.

examples.label: Sets region:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    </Value>
