      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MapView.SetRotateEnabled
functions.description: Enables or disables rotation. A Boolean value indicating whether the map camera’s heading information is used. When this property is set to 1 and a valid camera is associated with the map, the camera’s heading angle is used to rotate the plane of the map around its center point. When this property is set to false, the camera’s heading angle is ignored and the map is always oriented so that true north is situated at the top of the map view.

parameters.name: MapView
parameters.description: The map view reference number.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

functions.name: MapView.SetScript
functions.description: Sets script trigger. Script is called for pins with identifiers when clicked. With version 9.5, we start passing JSON with various values included. Version 9.3 and 9.4 only pass annotation identifier. The JSON includes mapViewID, annotationIdentifier, latitude, longitude, mouseInViewX, mouseInViewY, mouseInWindowX, mouseInWindowY, mouseLocationX, mouseLocationY, reason and overlayIdentifiers. overlayIdentifiers are found by bounding rectangles, so we don't check polygons or circles exactly! The JSON contains an entry reason for why the script was called including Touch, Click, AnnotationMoved, and AnnotationSelected. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: MapView
parameters.description: The map view reference number.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

examples.label: Set script:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$$MapView
; Value:
MBS
("
MapView.CreateWithControl
"; 0; "PlaceHolder") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("MapView.SetScript";
$$MapView
; Get(FileName); "PinClicked") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("MapView.CreateWithControl"; 0; "PlaceHolder") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$MapView</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("MapView.SetScript"; $$MapView; Get(FileName); "PinClicked") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MapView.SetScrollEnabled
functions.description: Enables or disables scrolling. A Boolean value that determines whether the user may scroll around the map.

parameters.name: MapView
parameters.description: The map view reference number.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

functions.name: MapView.SetShowsBuildings
functions.description: Shows or hides buildings. A Boolean indicating whether the map displays extruded building information. When this property is set to 1 and the camera has a pitch angle greater than zero, the map extrudes buildings so that they extend above the map plane, creating a 3D effect. The mapType property must be set to Standard for extruded buildings to be displayed. The default value of this property is 1.

parameters.name: MapView
parameters.description: The map view reference number.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

functions.name: MapView.SetShowsCompass
functions.description: Shows or hides compass. A Boolean indicating whether the map displays a compass control. Use this property to show or hide a control that lets users change the heading orientation of the map.

parameters.name: MapView
parameters.description: The map view reference number.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

functions.name: MapView.SetShowsPointsOfInterest
functions.description: Shows or hides points of interest. A Boolean indicating whether the map displays point-of-interest information. When this property is set to 1, the map displays icons and labels for restaurants, schools, and other relevant points of interest. The mapType property must be set to Standard or Hybrid for points of interest to be displayed.The default value of this property is 1.

parameters.name: MapView
parameters.description: The map view reference number.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

functions.name: MapView.SetShowsScale
functions.description: Sets whether to show scale. A Boolean indicating whether the map shows scale information.

parameters.name: MapView
parameters.description: The map view reference number.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

functions.name: MapView.SetShowsTraffic
functions.description: Enables or disables traffic. A Boolean value indicating whether the map displays traffic information.

parameters.name: MapView
parameters.description: The map view reference number.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

functions.name: MapView.SetShowsUserLocation
functions.description: Sets whether to show user location. A Boolean value indicating whether the map should try to display the user’s location. This property does not indicate whether the user’s position is actually visible on the map, only whether the map view should try to display it. Setting this property to 1 causes the map view to use the Core Location framework to find the current location and try to display it on the map. As long as this property is 1, the map view continues to track the user’s location and update it periodically. The default value of this property is 0. Showing the user’s location does not guarantee that the location is visible on the map. The user might have scrolled the map to a different point, causing the current location to be offscreen. To determine whether the user’s current location is currently displayed on the map, use the MapView.IsUserLocationVisible function.

parameters.name: MapView
parameters.description: The map view reference number.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

examples.label: Create map view and show user location:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$$MapView
; Value:
MBS
("
MapView.CreateWithControl
"; 0; "PlaceHolder") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MapView.SetShowsUserLocation";
$$MapView
; 1 ) ]
# may trigger dialog to ask for location permissions
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("MapView.CreateWithControl"; 0; "PlaceHolder") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$MapView</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MapView.SetShowsUserLocation"; $$MapView; 1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> may trigger dialog to ask for location permissions</Text>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MapView.SetShowsZoomControls
functions.description: Shows or hides zoom controls. A Boolean indicating whether the map displays zoom controls.

parameters.name: MapView
parameters.description: The map view reference number.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

functions.name: MapView.SetTag
functions.description: Sets the tag string for this map view. Use the tag to store whatever value you want to associate with the image view. You can later query it using MapView.GetTag .

parameters.name: MapView
parameters.description: The map view reference number.

parameters.name: Tag
parameters.description: A string you want to associate with the map view.

functions.name: MapView.SetToolTip
functions.description: Sets tooltip for map view. The tooltip is visible on Mac when mouse stays over control. There are no tooltips for iOS.

parameters.name: MapView
parameters.description: The map view reference number.

parameters.name: Tooltip
parameters.description: The new help text.

functions.name: MapView.SetVisible
functions.description: Hides or shows the map view. By default we create it visible, but you can hide it if it's on a register and the user clicked to other tab.

parameters.name: MapView
parameters.description: The map view reference number.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new visible state. Can be 1 for visible and 0 for hidden. Default is 1.

functions.name: MapView.SetVisibleMapRect
functions.description: Sets visible map rectangle. Changes the currently visible portion of the map and optionally animates the change.

parameters.name: MapView
parameters.description: The map view reference number.

parameters.name: Rect
parameters.description: The map rectangle. A JSON with x, y, width and height.

examples.label: Sets visible rect:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MapView.SetVisibleMapRect"; $$MapView; "{ \"x\" : 140455603.74390683, \"height\" : 799234.62509903312, \"y\" : 86194618.191567183, \"width\" : 1501801.5633610785}" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MapView.SetZoomEnabled
functions.description: Enables or disables zoom. A Boolean value that determines whether the user may use pinch gestures to zoom in and out of the map.

parameters.name: MapView
parameters.description: The map view reference number.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

functions.name: MapView.SetZoomLevel
functions.description: Sets zoom level.

parameters.name: MapView
parameters.description: The map view reference number.

parameters.name: ZoomLevel
parameters.description: The new zoom level. Range 1 to 20.

parameters.name: Animated
parameters.description: Whether to animate. Pass 1 to animate or 0 to not animate. Default is animated.

examples.label: Zoom one level in:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MapView.SetZoomLevel"; $$MapView; MBS( "MapView.GetZoomLevel"; $$MapView ) + 1; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Zoom out:

MBS
( "MapView.SetZoomLevel";
$$MapView
; 1; 1)
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MapView.SetZoomLevel"; $$MapView; 1; 1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MapView.ShowAddress
functions.signature: MBS( "MapView.ShowAddress"; MapView; Address { ; addPin; Identifier; Color } )
functions.description: Shows an address. Asynchronously queries coordinate for given address and shows it.

parameters.name: MapView
parameters.description: The map view reference number.

parameters.name: Address
parameters.description: The address to lookup."

parameters.name: addPin
parameters.description: Whether to add a pin. Pass 0 to just show the area or 1 to add pin and show the area.

parameters.name: Identifier
parameters.description: The identifier for the pin to use with script trigger.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: The color to use. Can be a color name or hex notation like in HTML.

examples.label: Show pin in New York in green:

MBS
("MapView.ShowAddress";
$$MapView
; "New York"; 1; ""; "#00FF00")

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MapView.ShowAllAnnotations
functions.description: Zooms map to show all annotations. The plugin loops over the annotations to calculate a bounding rectangle for all annotations and zoom the map to make them all visible with some border space around.

parameters.name: Overlays

functions.name: MapView.Snapshot
functions.description: Creates a map snapshot. Not sure if this works on server! Keys in the JSON include:

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: The options as JSON block. All values are optional and have default.

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

Snapshots::Image

examples.label: Snapshot with camera:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.CreateObjectRef
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.AddNumberToObject
";
$json
; "width"; 800 ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.AddNumberToObject
";
$json
; "height"; 600 ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.AddStringToObject
";
$json
; "mapType"; "HybridFlyover" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.AddNumberToObject
";
$json
; "showsBuildings"; 1 ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.AddNumberToObject
";
$json
; "showsPointsOfInterest"; 1 ) ]
#
Set Variable [
$camera
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.CreateObjectRef
" ) ]
# The coordinate point on which the map should be centered.
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.AddNumberToObject
";
$camera
; "latitude";
40.7457172
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.AddNumberToObject
";
$camera
; "longitude"; -
73.9941266
) ]
#
# The line-of-sight distance from the camera to the center coordinate of the map.
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.AddNumberToObject
";
$camera
; "distance"; 3000 ) ]
#
# The viewing angle of the camera, measured in degrees.
# A value of 0 results in a camera pointed straight down at the map. Angles greater than 0 result in a camera that is pitched toward the horizon by the specified number of degrees. If the map type is satellite or hybrid, the pitch value is clamped to 0.
# The value in this property may be clamped to a maximum value to maintain map readability. There is no fixed maximum value, though, because the actual maximum value is dependent on the current altitude of the camera.
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.AddNumberToObject
";
$camera
; "pitch"; 70 ) ]
#
# The heading of the camera (measured in degrees) relative to true north.
# The value 0 means that the top edge of the map view corresponds to true north. The value 90 means the top of the map is pointing due east. The value 180 means the top of the map points due south, and so on.
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.AddNumberToObject
";
$camera
; "heading"; 28 ) ]
#
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.AddItemToObject
";
$json
; "camera";
$camera
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.Release
";
$camera
) ]
#
Set Variable [
$options
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.Format
";
$json
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.Release
";
$json
) ]
Set Variable [
$image
; Value:
MBS
("MapView.Snapshot";
$options
; "PNG"; "map.png") ]
If [
MBS
("isError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to render map" ;
$image
]
Else
Set Field [
Snapshots::Image
;
$image
]
End If

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.CreateObjectRef" ) </Calculation>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>

      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.AddNumberToObject"; $camera; "latitude"; 40.7457172 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.AddNumberToObject"; $camera; "longitude"; -73.9941266 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> The line-of-sight distance from the camera to the center coordinate of the map.</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.AddNumberToObject"; $camera; "distance"; 3000 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> The viewing angle of the camera, measured in degrees.</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> A value of 0 results in a camera pointed straight down at the map. Angles greater than 0 result in a camera that is pitched toward the horizon by the specified number of degrees. If the map type is satellite or hybrid, the pitch value is clamped to 0.</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> The value in this property may be clamped to a maximum value to maintain map readability. There is no fixed maximum value, though, because the actual maximum value is dependent on the current altitude of the camera.</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.AddNumberToObject"; $camera; "pitch"; 70 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> The heading of the camera (measured in degrees) relative to true north.</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> The value 0 means that the top edge of the map view corresponds to true north. The value 90 means the top of the map is pointing due east. The value 180 means the top of the map points due south, and so on.</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

functions.name: Python.AddEvaluateFunction
functions.description: Adds an evaluate function. You can register a function to evaluate FileMaker expressions if you like to have that. You can pick the name to use, but we default to FMEvaluate. The Python function is then added locally and can be called. It takes one parameter with the expression to evaluate.

parameters.name: PythonRef
parameters.description: The Python reference number.

parameters.name: FunctionName
parameters.description: The function name to use. Default is "FMEvaluate".

examples.label: Try the evaluate function:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$p
; Value:
MBS
("
Python.New
") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Python.AddEvaluateFunction";
$p
; "FMEvaluate") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Python.Evaluate
";
$p
; "FMEvaluate('1+2')") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Result" ;
$r
]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Python.Release
";
$p
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Python.New") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$p</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Python.AddEvaluateFunction"; $p; "FMEvaluate") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Python.Evaluate"; $p; "FMEvaluate('1+2')") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Result" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Python.Release"; $p) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Python.AddExecuteSQLFunction
functions.description: Adds an execute SQL function. You can register a function to execute SQL in FileMaker if you like to have that. You can pick the name to use, but we default to FMExecuteSQL. The Python function is then added locally and can be called. It takes several parameters: SQL expression. Optionally the file name for the database. Optionally parameters as needed for the question marks in the SQL.

parameters.name: PythonRef
parameters.description: The Python reference number.

parameters.name: FunctionName
parameters.description: The function name to use. Default is "FMExecuteSQL".

examples.label: Query SQL from Python:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$p
; Value:
MBS
("
Python.New
") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Python.AddExecuteSQLFunction";
$p
; "FMExecuteSQL") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Python.Evaluate
";
$p
; "FMExecuteSQL('SELECT PrimaryKey FROM Python')") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "SQL Result:" ;
$r
]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Python.Release
";
$p
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Python.New") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$p</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Python.AddExecuteSQLFunction"; $p; "FMExecuteSQL") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Python.Evaluate"; $p; "FMExecuteSQL('SELECT PrimaryKey FROM Python')") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "SQL Result:" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Python.Release"; $p) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.name: PythonRef

functions.name: Python.Evaluate
functions.description: Evaluates an expression in Python. Pass an expression to evaluate with Python. Returns the result of the expression. The evaluate runs within the FileMaker process. If you cause an endless loop, FileMaker freezes and needs to be force quit, so please always include an exit condition for your loops. Also if you get Python to crash, FileMaker will crash with it. Use Shell functions to run Python in a separate process.

parameters.name: PythonRef
parameters.description: The Python reference number.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The Python expression.

examples.label: Try it:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Python.Evaluate";
$p
; "1+2") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") or
$r
≠ 3 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to evaluate" ;
$r
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Python.Evaluate"; $p; "1+2") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") or $r ≠ 3 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to evaluate" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result:    </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Python.LibraryPlatform
functions.description: Return the platform identifier for the current platform. On Unix, this is formed from the "official" name of the operating system, converted to lower case, followed by the major revision number; e.g., for Solaris 2.x, which is also known as SunOS 5.x, the value is 'sunos5'. On macOS, it is 'darwin'. On Windows, it is 'win'. The value is available to Python code as sys.platform.

functions.name: Python.New
functions.description: Creates a new Python environment.

functions.name: Python.Path
functions.description: Queries the default module search path. This is computed from the program name (set by assigning ProgramName) and some environment variables. The returned string consists of a series of directory names separated by a platform dependent delimiter character. The delimiter character is ':' on Unix and macOS, ';' on Windows. The list sys.path is initialized with this value on interpreter startup; it can be (and usually is) modified later to change the search path for loading modules.

examples.label: Query path on macOS:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Python.Path" )
Example result:
/opt/homebrew/Cellar/python@3.12/3.12.3/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.12/lib/python312.zip:/opt/homebrew/Cellar/python@3.12/3.12.3/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.12/lib/python3.12:/opt/homebrew/Cellar/python@3.12/3.12.3/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.12/lib/python3.12/lib-dynload

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Python.PrintOutput

parameters.name: PythonRef

functions.name: Python.ProgramFullPath
functions.description: Queries full program path. Return the full program name of the Python executable; this is computed as a side-effect of deriving the default module search path from the program name (set by Python.SetProgramName ). The value is available to Python code as sys.executable. If called in FileMaker will show you the path to the FileMaker app.

functions.name: Python.ProgramName

functions.name: Python.PythonHome

functions.name: Python.Release
functions.description: Releases the Python environment.

parameters.name: PythonRef
parameters.description: The Python reference number.

functions.name: Python.ReleaseAll
functions.description: Releases all Python environments. Be aware that on a server this may free objects, which are used by other scripts running in parallel!

functions.name: Python.Run
functions.description: Runs a Python script. Pass the script text to run. Returns the result of the script. Before running a specific script, you may run a prefix script to setup sys.argv with the arguments you like to have. Python.SetValue can help to set a few global variables. The script runs within the FileMaker process. If you cause an endless loop, FileMaker freezes and needs to be force quit, so please always include an exit condition for your loops. Also if you get Python to crash, FileMaker will crash with it. Use Shell functions to run Python in a separate process. You may want to use Process.SetCurrentDirectory function to set the current directory, so local files are found.

parameters.name: PythonRef
parameters.description: The Python reference number.

parameters.name: ScriptText
parameters.description: The script text.

examples.label: Try it:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Python.Run";
$p
; "x = 1¶if x == 1:¶    # indented four spaces¶    print('x is 1.')") ]
Set Variable [
$o
; Value:
MBS
("
Python.PrintOutput
";
$p
) ]
If [
MBS
("
IsError
") or
$o
≠ "x is 1.¶" ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to evaluate" ;
$r
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$o</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") or $o ≠ "x is 1.¶" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to evaluate" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result:    </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Python.RunFile
functions.description: Runs a Python script file. Same as Python.Run , but points to a file to run. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: PythonRef
parameters.description: The Python reference number.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native path to the file.

functions.name: Python.SetInput
functions.description: Sets the input values to use.

parameters.name: Input
parameters.description: A list of values to return. Each call to input() function returns one line.

functions.name: Python.SetProgramName
functions.description: Sets the program name. Can be set before initializing Python, otherwise the default will be "python". This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The new program name.

functions.name: Python.SetPythonHome
functions.description: Sets the Python home. The value set by a previous call to Python.SetPythonHome, or the value of the PYTHONHOME environment variable if it is set. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The new python home path.

examples.label: Set the home path:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Python.SetPythonHome"; "/opt/mycustompython" )
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Python.SetPythonHome"; "/opt/mycustompython" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Python.SetRecursionLimit
functions.description: Sets the recursion limit. Default is 1000.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new recursion limit.

functions.name: Python.SetTag
functions.description: Sets the tag value. The tag value allows you to associate any value with the phidget, e.g. a record ID. See Python.GetTag to query value later.

parameters.name: PythonRef
parameters.description: The Python reference number.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new tag value.

functions.name: Python.SetValue
functions.description: Sets a variable in the Python environment. You may use the variable to easily transfer huge blocks of JSON text to process in Python. Use Python.GetValue later to query value back.

parameters.name: PythonRef
parameters.description: The Python reference number.

parameters.name: VariableName
parameters.description: The variable name.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to set.

      <Calculation>MBS("Python.SetValue"; $p; "Hello"; "World") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Python.Time
functions.description: Read the "wall clock" time. Return the time in seconds since the epoch as a floating-point number. The handling of leap seconds is platform dependent. On Windows and most Unix systems, the leap seconds are not counted towards the time in seconds since the epoch. This is commonly referred to as Unix time.

examples.label: Query the time:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Python.Time" )

1743330201673808000

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: QLPreviewPanel.AddFile
functions.description: Adds file to the file list for preview. If you want the user to select a path, please use FileDialog functions. A QLPreviewController can display previews for many common file types, including the following: iWork documents Microsoft Office documents Rich text format, or RTF, documents PDF files Images Text files with a uniform type identifier that conforms to the public.text type. To learn more, see Uniform type identifiers. Comma-separated values, or CSV, files 3D models in the USDZ format with both standalone and AR views for viewing the model The list of supported common file types may change between operating system releases. In addition, you can enable previews for your own file types by implementing your own preview extension. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path to the image file.

functions.name: QLPreviewPanel.AddImage
functions.description: Adds an image to the list of files to preview. If you have an image file, better use QLPreviewPanel.AddFile function. The container can contain an image (GIF, PNG, BMP, JPEG) or an image file (TIFF, BMP, PNG, JPEG and other formats supported by macOS). Either we use file path from FileMaker's external container storage directly or we write a temporary file, which is cleared later when app quits or panel is cleared.

parameters.name: Image
parameters.description: The image as container.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: The title for the image.

functions.name: QLPreviewPanel.AddPDF
functions.description: Adds a PDF to the preview items. You can pass a container value with the PDF content from a media field. Or a text with an URL. Or a PDF reference from PDFKit.Open . Either we use file path from FileMaker's external container storage directly or we write a temporary file, which is cleared later when app quits or panel is cleared.

parameters.name: PDF
parameters.description: The PDF.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: The title to use.

functions.name: QLPreviewPanel.Clear
functions.description: Clears content of file list. Please call this function always before you start to fill This also deletes any temporary file created by QLPreviewPanel.AddPDF or QLPreviewPanel.AddImage .

functions.name: QLPreviewPanel.CurrentPreviewItemIndex
functions.description: Queries index of current visible item. Can be empty if no index is set.

functions.name: QLPreviewPanel.CurrentPreviewItemPath
functions.description: Queries path of current item.

functions.name: QLPreviewPanel.CurrentPreviewItemTitle
functions.description: Queries title of current item.

functions.name: QLPreviewPanel.GetEditingAllowed
functions.description: Queries whether editing is allowed.

functions.name: QLPreviewPanel.GetHeight
functions.description: Queries height of panel.

functions.name: QLPreviewPanel.GetLeft
functions.description: Queries left position of panel.

functions.name: QLPreviewPanel.GetSharingAllowed
functions.description: Queries whether sharing is allowed. By default is this enabled. Use QLPreviewPanel.SetSharingAllowed to change setting.

functions.name: QLPreviewPanel.GetTop
functions.description: Queries height of panel.

functions.name: QLPreviewPanel.GetWidth
functions.description: Queries width of panel.

functions.name: QLPreviewPanel.Hide
functions.description: Hides the panel. Added iOS support for version 8.4.

functions.name: QLPreviewPanel.IsVisible
functions.description: Checks if panel is visible. Added iOS support for version 8.4.
functions.result: Returns 1 if visible or 0 if not.

functions.name: QLPreviewPanel.Move
functions.description: Moves the panel to the given position. You can use this method to have the panel side by side to your FileMaker layout window.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: The new screen position. Distance from top/left edge of main screen in points.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: The new screen position. Distance from top/Left edge of main screen in points.

parameters.name: Duration
parameters.description: Optional the duration. Can be a value between 0.1 and 5.0. Use zero to disable.

functions.name: QLPreviewPanel.Resize
functions.description: Resizes the panel.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The new width of the panel.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: The new height of the panel.

parameters.name: NoAnimation
parameters.description: Whether to avoid animation. Pass 1 to disable animation. Default is 0 to allow animation.

parameters.name: Duration
parameters.description: Only for Mac: the duration. Can be a value between 0.1 and 5.0.

functions.name: QLPreviewPanel.SavedFiles
functions.description: Queries list of file paths. The saved files may have some random UUID based file name, so please keep original file name at hand. When reading back file, you may reuse the file name.

examples.label: Script triggered when panel closed:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:
MBS
("QLPreviewPanel.SavedFiles") ]
Set Variable [
$count
; Value: ValueCount(
$list
) ]
#
If [
$count
> 0 ]
# a document was saved
Set Variable [
$path
; Value: GetValue(
$list
; 1) ]
# read file
Set Variable [
$file
; Value:
MBS
( "
Container.ReadFile
";
$path
; "auto") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Set Field [
AVRecorder::Image
;
$file
]
End If
# cleanup
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Files.Delete
";
$Path
) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("QLPreviewPanel.SavedFiles") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>ValueCount($list) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $count > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> a document was saved</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue($list; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> read file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.ReadFile"; $path; "auto") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$file</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$file </Calculation>
    <Field table="AVRecorder" id="99999" name="Image "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.Delete"; $Path ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: QLPreviewPanel.SetDismissTrigger
functions.description: Sets the script trigger. This script is called when a panel is dismissed. Added macOS support in plugin version 16.2.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run.

parameters.name: Parameter
parameters.description: The parameter to pass to the script.

examples.label: Setup trigger:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "QLPreviewPanel.SetDismissTrigger"; Get(FileName); "Dismissed") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
QLPreviewPanel.Show
") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to show panel" ;
$r
]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "QLPreviewPanel.SetDismissTrigger"; Get(FileName); "Dismissed") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "QLPreviewPanel.Show") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to show panel" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: QLPreviewPanel.Show
functions.description: Shows the panel. Added iOS support for version 8.4. See also Icon.GetIconWithQuickLook for just getting the preview as image.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Pass various flags. Add 1 to turn fullscreen. Add 2 to hide top right buttons.

examples.label: Show previews:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
QLPreviewPanel.Clear
") ]
# Add all items
Go to Record/Request/Page [ First ]
Loop
If [ Length(
QuickLook Preview Panel::FilePath
) > 0 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
QLPreviewPanel.AddFile
";
QuickLook Preview Panel::FilePath
) ]
End If
If [ not IsEmpty(
QuickLook Preview Panel::ImageContainer
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
QLPreviewPanel.AddImage
";
QuickLook Preview Panel::ImageContainer
) ]
End If
If [ not IsEmpty(
QuickLook Preview Panel::PDFContainer
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
QLPreviewPanel.AddPDF
";
QuickLook Preview Panel::PDFContainer
) ]
End If
Go to Record/Request/Page [ Next; Exit after last ]
End Loop
# Run it!
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("QLPreviewPanel.Show") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Add all items</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="16" name="Go to Record/Request/Page">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <RowPageLocation value="First"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Length(QuickLook Preview Panel::FilePath) > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("QLPreviewPanel.AddFile"; QuickLook Preview Panel::FilePath) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>

    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> not IsEmpty(QuickLook Preview Panel::ImageContainer) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("QLPreviewPanel.AddImage"; QuickLook Preview Panel::ImageContainer) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> not IsEmpty(QuickLook Preview Panel::PDFContainer) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("QLPreviewPanel.AddPDF"; QuickLook Preview Panel::PDFContainer) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>

  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="16" name="Go to Record/Request/Page">

    <Exit state="True"/>
    <RowPageLocation value="Next"/>

functions.name: QuickList.AddSQL
functions.signature: MBS( "QuickList.AddSQL"; ListRef; SQL Statement; FileName { ; Params... } )
functions.description: Adds values to a quick list via SQL. All values in the result set are added as text to the list. Returns error in case of SQL error with error message. This function is currently only supported for FileMaker 11 and newer. If you need it for older versions, please contact us. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Params parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: SQL Statement
parameters.description: SQL Statement as a Text string

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The target database name. Can be empty to not limit query to one database.

parameters.name: Params...
parameters.description: Optional, pass here parameters. One parameter to this function for each parameter you want to pass to the SQL statement.

functions.result: Returns number of items added or error.

examples.label: Fill a list and return it:
examples.text_code:
# create new list
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:
MBS
("
QuickList.New
") ]
# add values via SQL
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("QuickList.AddSQL";
$list
; "SELECT TextField FROM \"Test Table\"")]
# query list content for displaying
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:
MBS
("
QuickList.GetList
";
$list
) ]
# and free it
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
QuickList.Release
";
$list
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> create new list</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("QuickList.New") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> add values via SQL</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("QuickList.AddSQL"; $list; "SELECT TextField FROM \"Test Table\"")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> query list content for displaying</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("QuickList.GetList"; $list) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and free it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("QuickList.Release"; $list )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: QuickList.And
functions.description: Returns a list with values which are contained in both lists. Performs a logical AND operation. This function is made for lists without duplicate values as it checks if values from one list exists in others, which may not work correctly if one list has duplicate values.

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The reference to the first quicklist.

parameters.name: List2
parameters.description: The reference to the second quicklist.

parameters.name: ReturnNewList
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. This new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to modify the list in the List1 parameter.

examples.label: Performs And on two quicklists:
examples.text_code:
$list1
=
MBS
( "
QuickList.New
"; "a¶b¶c¶d")
$list2
=
MBS
( "
QuickList.New
"; "a¶d")
$list3
=
MBS
( "QuickList.And";
$list1
;
$list2
; 1 )
$list
=
MBS
( "
QuickList.GetList
";
$list3
)
$r
=
MBS
("
QuickList.Release
";
$list1
)
$r
=
MBS
("
QuickList.Release
";
$list2
)
$r
=
MBS
("
QuickList.Release
";
$list3
)

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list3</Name>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

functions.name: QuickList.AndColumn
functions.signature: MBS( "QuickList.AndColumn"; List1; List2; Separator; Column { ; ReturnNewList } )
functions.description: Returns a list with values which are contained in both lists. Performs a logical AND operation. Checks the given column in both lists too compare. If Separator is empty, this is same as QuickList.And , but slower.

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The reference to the first quicklist.

parameters.name: List2
parameters.description: The reference to the second quicklist.

parameters.name: Separator
parameters.description: The separator text. Can be multiple characters. For tab separated text, pass Char(9). For CSV separated text, pass ";" or ",".

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The index of the column to compare. Starts with 0 for first column.

parameters.name: ReturnNewList
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. This new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to modify the list in the List1 parameter.

functions.name: QuickList.BestMatch
functions.description: Looks for best match of a text in a list of texts. If not found, returns empty result, so FileMaker function isEmpty() will tell you if it's empty (instead of just having no text). Uses internally the Text.LevenshteinDistance function to compare all items and look for best match. If no entry matches over 50%, this may return empty text.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: SearchText
parameters.description: The text to search.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Pass 0 to use Levenshtein distance. Pass 1 to use Jaro-Winkler distance. Add 2 to return list of top 5 entries.

functions.result: Returns best match, empty or error.

functions.name: QuickList.CSVSplit
functions.description: Splits CSV text. Plugin automatically detects if comma or semicolon is used. Returns list with all values. Version 8 can detect tab character, too.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The CSV text.

parameters.name: Delimiter
parameters.description: The delimiter to use. If empty, we auto detect it.

functions.result: Returns QuickList reference number or error.

examples.label: Try with 3 values:
examples.text_code:
MBS
("QuickList.CSVSplit"; "\"hello\";\"test\";\"CSV\"")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("QuickList.CSVSplit"; "\"hello\";\"test\";\"CSV\"")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: QuickList.Clear
functions.description: Clears the content of the list.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

functions.name: QuickList.Clone
functions.description: Creates a clone of the list. A new reference number with a new list object or array behind and all content copied.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The reference to the quicklist.

functions.name: QuickList.Count
functions.description: Queries the count of items in list.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

functions.name: QuickList.CountValuesMatching
functions.description: Count matching entries in list. This uses text comparison!

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: Operator
parameters.description: The compare operator. Can be =, !=, <, >, <=, >=, ≤, ≥ or ≠.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to check against.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: Pass 1 for numeric comparison. Default is 0 for text comparison.

examples.label: Count values in a quick list <4:
examples.text_code:
Let([
list =
MBS
( "
QuickList.New
"; "1¶2¶3¶4");
count =
MBS
( "QuickList.CountValuesMatching"; list; "<"; 4 );
r =
MBS
("
QuickList.Release
"; list)];
count)
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
list = MBS( "QuickList.New"; "1¶2¶3¶4");
count = MBS( "QuickList.CountValuesMatching"; list; "<"; 4 );
r = MBS("QuickList.Release"; list)];
count)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: QuickList.CrossProduct
functions.signature: MBS( "QuickList.CrossProduct"; List1; List2 { ; Prefix; Midfix; Postfix; Flags } )
functions.description: Creates the cross product from two lists. Optionally adds prefix, postfix and midfix. Returns empty list if one of the lists are empty!

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The reference to the first quicklist.

parameters.name: List2
parameters.description: The reference to the second quicklist.

parameters.name: Prefix
parameters.description: The prefix to use

parameters.name: Midfix
parameters.description: The middle to use. This text is put between texts from both lists.

parameters.name: Postfix
parameters.description: The postfix to use

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. This new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to modify the list in the List1 parameter. Add 2 for a diagonal cross, so we combine each element of first list with the same element index from the second list.

examples.label: Try in a Let statement:
examples.text_code:
Let([
list1 =
MBS
("
QuickList.New
"; "Hello¶World");
list2 =
MBS
("
QuickList.New
"; "1¶2");
e =
MBS
("QuickList.CrossProduct"; list1; list2);
r =
MBS
("
QuickList.GetList
"; list1);
d =
MBS
("
QuickList.Release
"; list1);
d =
MBS
("
QuickList.Release
"; list2)
]; r)
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
list1 = MBS("QuickList.New"; "Hello¶World");
list2 = MBS("QuickList.New"; "1¶2");
e = MBS("QuickList.CrossProduct"; list1; list2);
r = MBS("QuickList.GetList"; list1);
d = MBS("QuickList.Release"; list1);
d = MBS("QuickList.Release"; list2)
]; r)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: QuickList.DeCombine
functions.signature: MBS( "QuickList.DeCombine"; ListRef; ColumnDelimiter; StartColumn; EndColumn { ; ReturnNewList; NoReturnEnding } )
functions.description: Picks elements from each line in a list. This function combines values from a list by picking only given columns.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: ColumnDelimiter
parameters.description: The delimiter between colums. This is also used in result.

parameters.name: StartColumn
parameters.description: Index of column to start. First is 0.

parameters.name: EndColumn
parameters.description: Index of column to end. First is 0. If you only want one column, please pass same value for start and end column.

parameters.name: ReturnNewList
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. This new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to return a text list.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: If you passed 0 for ReturnNewList, decides about new list: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

examples.label: Sort a list column:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$List
; Value: "65412|Schreiber|Klaus|459oz8235" & ¶ & "76542|Abraham|Hermmann|38957zf" & ¶ & "85112|Behrens|Robert|489748hj" ]
Set Variable [
$QuickList1
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.New
";
$list
) ]
#
Set Variable [
$QuickList2
; Value:
MBS
( "QuickList.DeCombine";
$QuickList1
; "|"; 1 /* StartColumn*/; 1 /* EndColumn */; 1 /* New List*/ ) ]
Set Variable [
$List2
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.GetList
";
$QuickList2
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.SortWith
";
$QuickList2
; 1;
$QuickList1
) ]
Set Variable [
$List1
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.GetList
";
$QuickList1
) ]
#
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.Release
";
$QuickList1
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.Release
";
$QuickList2
) ]

functions.name: QuickList.DeleteColumn
functions.description: Queries a column from each line in a list.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The reference to the quicklist.

parameters.name: Separator
parameters.description: The separator text. Can be multiple characters. For tab separated text, pass Char(9). For CSV separated text, pass ";" or ",".

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The index of the column to compare. Starts with 0 for first column.

parameters.name: ReturnNewList
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. This new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to modify the list in the List parameter.

examples.label: Try the function by deleting a column:
examples.text_code:
Let([
list =
MBS
("
QuickList.New
"; "1-a-A¶2-b-B¶3-c-C"; ¶);
r =
MBS
("QuickList.DeleteColumn"; list; "-"; 1);
text =
MBS
("
QuickList.GetList
"; list);
r =
MBS
("
QuickList.Release
"; list)
]; text)
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
  list = MBS("QuickList.New"; "1-a-A¶2-b-B¶3-c-C"; ¶);
  r = MBS("QuickList.DeleteColumn"; list; "-"; 1);
  text = MBS("QuickList.GetList"; list);
  r = MBS("QuickList.Release"; list)
]; text)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: QuickList.Deserialize
functions.description: Restores a serialized list.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The serialized data as text.

functions.result: Returns quick list reference number or error.

examples.label: Create and save a list to restore it:

Set Variable [
$list
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.New
"; "Hello¶World¶1234¶Just a test!")]
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:

("
QuickList.Serialize
";
$list
)]
Set Variable [
$SecondList
; Value:
MBS
("Quicklist.Deserialize";
$text
)]
Show Custom Dialog ["List"; "Encoded: " &
$text
& ¶ & ¶ & "Decoded: " &
MBS
("
QuickList.GetList
";
$secondList
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
QuickList.Release
";
$list
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
QuickList.Release
";
$secondList
)]
examples.xml_code:

functions.name: QuickList.Find
functions.description: Finds an entry in the list. Returns -1 if item is not found.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to look for.

parameters.name: ignoreCase
parameters.description: Boolean to define whether to ignore case. Default is 0 for case sensitive compare. Pass 1 for case insensitive compare.

functions.name: QuickList.FindDuplicateItems
functions.description: Finds duplicate items in the list. Returns list with all duplicate items, but only counts each one once. Similar to QuickList.RemoveDuplicateItems and see also List.FindDuplicateItems .

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: ReturnNewList
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. This new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to modify the list in the List1 parameter.

parameters.name: CaseInsensitive
parameters.description: Whether to perform comparison case insensitve. By default we do case sensitive operations, which are faster. Pass 1 for insensitve or 0 for sensitive.

functions.name: QuickList.FindEquals
functions.description: Finds equal items in both lists.

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The reference to the first quicklist.

parameters.name: List2
parameters.description: The reference to the second quicklist.

parameters.name: ReturnNewList
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. This new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to modify the list in the List1 parameter.

functions.name: QuickList.FindUnequals
functions.description: Finds unequal items in both lists.

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The reference to the first quicklist.

parameters.name: List2
parameters.description: The reference to the second quicklist.

parameters.name: ReturnNewList
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. This new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to modify the list in the List1 parameter.

parameters.name: NotFromSecondList
parameters.description: Pass 1 to only returns items from first list not present in second list. Default is 0 to get items from both lists.

functions.name: QuickList.GetColumn
functions.description: Queries a column from each line in a list.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The reference to the quicklist.

parameters.name: Separator
parameters.description: The separator text. Can be multiple characters. For tab separated text, pass Char(9). For CSV separated text, pass ";" or ",".

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The index of the column to compare. Starts with 0 for first column.

parameters.name: ReturnNewList
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. This new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to modify the list in the List parameter.

examples.label: Try it:
examples.text_code:
Let ([
// new list
list =
MBS
("
QuickList.New
"; "Hello,123,abc¶World,456,def");
// query the column and copy to new list
col =
MBS
( "QuickList.GetColumn"; list; ","; 1; 1);
// now get text from list
r =
MBS
( "
QuickList.GetList
"; col);
// and free both lists
f =
MBS
( "
QuickList.Release
"; list);
f =
MBS
( "
QuickList.Release
"; col)
]; r )
Example result:
123
456
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ([
// new list
list = MBS("QuickList.New"; "Hello,123,abc¶World,456,def");
// query the column and copy to new list
col = MBS( "QuickList.GetColumn"; list; ","; 1; 1);
// now get text from list
r = MBS( "QuickList.GetList"; col);
// and free both lists
f = MBS( "QuickList.Release"; list);
f = MBS( "QuickList.Release"; col)
]; r )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: QuickList.GetList
functions.description: Queries the list as text. Lines are separated by return character.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

parameters.name: Delimiter
parameters.description: The delimiter to use. Can be empty to have no delimiter. Common use is to pass "¶", ", " or "; ".

examples.label: Create a list and query it:

examples.label: Build a 10000 entry List:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
q = MBS("QuickList.New");
// add 10000 entries
t = While ( [ i = 0 ] ; i < 10000 ; [ i = i + 1; r = MBS( "QuickList.AddValue"; q; "test " & i ) ] ; 0 );
// get as text
r = MBS("QuickList.GetList"; q);
// free memory
f = MBS("QuickList.Release"; q)
];
Length(r) & ¶ & r )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: QuickList.GetValue
functions.description: Queries a value in the list. Using our QuickList functions is much faster than using GetValue(listOfValues;valueNumber) function in FileMaker for large lists. If you enable tracing with Trace command, this function will be much slower due to the debug output.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the value to get. Must be in range from 0 to QuickList.Count -1.

examples.label: Query values from List:
examples.text_code:
$listID
=
MBS
( "
QuickList.New
"; "Hello¶World")
$v1
=
MBS
( "QuickList.GetValue";
$listID
; 1)
$v2
=
MBS
( "QuickList.GetValue";
$listID
; 2)
$r
=
MBS
("
QuickList.Release
";
$listID
)

functions.name: QuickList.HasDuplicateItems
functions.description: Checks if list has duplicates. Returns 1 if duplicate lines are found or 0 if not.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: CaseInsensitive
parameters.description: Whether to perform comparison case insensitve. By default we do case sensitive operations, which are faster. Pass 1 for insensitve or 0 for sensitive.

functions.name: QuickList.HasValue
functions.description: Checks whether a list contains a given value. Returns 1 if value is found or 0 if not. Text comparison is case sensitive.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to check.

examples.label: Checks if list contains value:
examples.text_code:

$q
; Value:
MBS
("
QuickList.New
"; "Hello¶World")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Contains World";
MBS
("QuickList.HasValue";
$q
; "World")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Contains Test";
MBS
("QuickList.HasValue";
$q
;  "Test")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
QuickList.Release
";
$q
)]

functions.name: QuickList.HasValues

parameters.name: ListRef

parameters.name: Values
parameters.description: The values to check.

examples.label: Check if a list has values:

list =
MBS
("
QuickList.New
"; "Hello¶World¶123");
found =

r =

("
QuickList.Release
"; list)
]; found)

functions.name: QuickList.IndexOfFirstValueMatching
functions.signature: MBS( "QuickList.IndexOfFirstValueMatching"; ListRef; Operator; Value { ; Mode; StartRow } )
functions.description: Finds index of first value matching value. This uses text comparison!

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: Operator
parameters.description: The compare operator. Can be =, !=, <, >, <=, >=, ≤, ≥, ≠, Prefix or Postfix.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to compare.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: Pass 1 for numeric comparison. Default is 0 for text comparison.

parameters.name: StartRow
parameters.description: The row to start at. Default is 0.

examples.label: Find index of first value ≥4:
examples.text_code:
Let([
list =
MBS
( "
QuickList.New
"; "1¶2¶3¶4");
count =
MBS
( "QuickList.IndexOfFirstValueMatching"; list; "≥"; 4 );
r =
MBS
("
QuickList.Release
"; list)];
count)
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>

list = MBS( "QuickList.New"; "1¶2¶3¶4");
count = MBS( "QuickList.IndexOfFirstValueMatching"; list; "≥"; 4 );
r = MBS("QuickList.Release"; list)];
count)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.name: Text

functions.name: QuickList.JoinJSON
functions.description: Joins text in a QuickList as JSON array. Values in the QuickList must be valid JSON. See JSON.GetArrayItemsAsQuickList function to split JSON to a QuickList.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The reference to the quicklist.

examples.label: Try the function by adding three values:
examples.text_code:
Let([
list =
MBS
( "
QuickList.New
" );
r =
MBS
( "
QuickList.AddValue
"; list; "{\"id\": 1}" );
r =
MBS
( "
QuickList.AddValue
"; list; "{\"id\": 2}" );
r =
MBS
( "
QuickList.AddValue
"; list; "{\"id\": 3}" );
result =
MBS
( "QuickList.JoinJSON"; list );
r =
MBS
( "
QuickList.Release
"; list )
]; result)
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
list = MBS( "QuickList.New" );
r = MBS( "QuickList.AddValue"; list; "{\"id\": 1}" );
r = MBS( "QuickList.AddValue"; list; "{\"id\": 2}" );
r = MBS( "QuickList.AddValue"; list; "{\"id\": 3}" );
result = MBS( "QuickList.JoinJSON"; list );
r = MBS( "QuickList.Release"; list )
]; result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Filter JSON items:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$json
; Value: "[{\"id\": 1},{\"id\": 2},{\"id\": 3}]" ]
# turn into a list
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.GetArrayItemsAsQuickList
";
$json
) ]
#
# now loop over entries to filter
Set Variable [
$n
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.Count
";
$list
) ]
If [
$n
> 0 ]
Loop
# get next item
Set Variable [
$n
; Value:
$n
- 1 ]
Set Variable [
$item
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.GetValue
";
$list
;
$n
) ]
#
# if meets criteria, then remove it
If [ JSONGetElement (
$item
; "id" ) = 2 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.Remove
";
$list
;
$n
) ]
End If
#
# next
Exit Loop If [
$n
≤ 0 ]
End Loop
End If
#
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "QuickList.JoinJSON";
$list
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "after filter" ;
$json
]
#
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.Release
";
$list
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"[{\"id\": 1},{\"id\": 2},{\"id\": 3}]" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> turn into a list</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.GetArrayItemsAsQuickList"; $json ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now loop over entries to filter</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "QuickList.Count"; $list ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$n</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $n > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get next item</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$n - 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$n</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "QuickList.GetValue"; $list; $n ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$item</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> if meets criteria, then remove it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> JSONGetElement ( $item ; "id" ) = 2 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "QuickList.Remove"; $list; $n ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $n ≤ 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "QuickList.JoinJSON"; $list ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "after filter" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$json </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "QuickList.Release"; $list) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: QuickList.MatchesPostfix
functions.description: Finds list entries which have a given postfix text. Comparison is case insensitive.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to find.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. his new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to modify the list in the List1 parameter. Add 2 to split. This is only useful with first option together (so pass 3 for both). Matching entries will be in new list and non matching entries will stay in old list. Add 4 to reverse operation (NOT).

$list
