parameters.description: The text to find.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. This new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to modify the list in the List1 parameter. Add 2 to split. This is only useful with first option together (so pass 3 for both). Matching entries will be in new list and non matching entries will stay in old list. Add 4 to reverse operation (NOT).

";
$list

";
$matchesList

& ¶ &  "Others: " &

functions.name: QuickList.New
functions.description: Creates a new list. You can provide whatever separator you need. Default is new line characters. But works also fine with comma, semicolon or tab characters. Please use QuickList.Release to release the list later. QuickList reference numbers are starting at 12000 and counting up for each new list. QuickLists can have multiple million of entries without problems. Just be aware of FileMaker's standard loop/recursion limit of 50000, which can be raised with SetRecursion function.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: Initial list content.

parameters.name: Separator
parameters.description: The separator to use. Can be ASCII character number (e.g. 9 for tab) or a text where we use first character as separator. Default is newline.

parameters.name: RefNum
parameters.description: The desired reference number. Leave empty for default.

examples.label: Creates new list:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "QuickList.New"; "Hello¶World¶Test" )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "QuickList.New"; "Hello¶World¶Test" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

QuickList.GetList

    </Repetition>

functions.name: QuickList.Not
functions.description: Returns values of first list which are not in second list. Performs a logical NOT operation.

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The reference to the first quicklist.

parameters.name: List2
parameters.description: The reference to the second quicklist.

parameters.name: ReturnNewList
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. This new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to modify the list in the List1 parameter.

functions.name: QuickList.NotColumn
functions.signature: MBS( "QuickList.NotColumn"; List1; List2; Separator; Column { ; ReturnNewList } )
functions.description: Returns values of first list which are not in second list. Performs a logical NOT operation. Checks the given column in both lists too compare. If Separator is empty, this is same as QuickList.Not , but slower.

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The reference to the first quicklist.

parameters.name: List2
parameters.description: The reference to the second quicklist.

parameters.name: Separator
parameters.description: The separator text. Can be multiple characters. For tab separated text, pass Char(9). For CSV separated text, pass ";" or ",".

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The index of the column to compare. Starts with 0 for first column.

parameters.name: ReturnNewList
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. This new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to modify the list in the List1 parameter.

functions.name: QuickList.Or
functions.description: Returns values of both lists. Performs a logical OR operation.

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The reference to the first quicklist.

parameters.name: List2
parameters.description: The reference to the second quicklist.

parameters.name: ReturnNewList
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. This new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to modify the list in the List1 parameter.

examples.label: Creates two lists and combines them:
examples.text_code:
$list1
=
MBS
( "
QuickList.New
"; "Hello¶World")
$list2
=
MBS
( "
QuickList.New
"; "a¶b")
$list3
=
MBS
( "QuickList.Or";
$list1
;
$list2
; 1 )
$list
=
MBS
( "
QuickList.GetList
";
$list3
)
$r
=
MBS
("
QuickList.Release
";
$list1
)
$r
=
MBS
("
QuickList.Release
";
$list2
)
$r
=
MBS
("
QuickList.Release
";
$list3
)
examples.xml_code:

    </Repetition>

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("QuickList.Release"; $list2)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

      <Calculation>MBS("QuickList.Release"; $list3)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

functions.name: QuickList.OrColumn

functions.description: Returns values of both lists. Performs a logical OR operation. Checks the given column in both lists too compare. If Separator is empty, this is same as QuickList.Or , but slower.

parameters.name: List1

parameters.name: List2

parameters.name: Separator
parameters.description: The separator text. Can be multiple characters. For tab separated text, pass Char(9). For CSV separated text, pass ";" or ",".

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The index of the column to compare. Starts with 0 for first column.

parameters.name: ReturnNewList
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. This new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to modify the list in the List1 parameter.

functions.name: QuickList.RegExMatch
functions.signature: MBS( "QuickList.RegExMatch"; ListRef; Pattern; CompilerOptions; ExecuteOptions { ; ReturnNewList } )
functions.description: Matches a list against a pattern. This compiles the pattern, executes it and looks if we have a match or not for each list item. Returns the list items which match.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: Pattern
parameters.description: The pattern to use.

parameters.name: CompilerOptions
parameters.description: A number for the options to use. e.g. 1 for case insensitive or 512 for ungreedy.

parameters.name: ExecuteOptions
parameters.description: The options for executing.

parameters.name: ReturnNewList
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. This new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to modify the list in the List1 parameter.

functions.name: QuickList.Release
functions.description: Releases the list. Please call this for each list to make sure you don't run out of memory.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

examples.label: Creates list and frees it:
examples.text_code:
$list
=
MBS
( "
QuickList.New
"; "Hello¶World¶Test" )
$r
=
MBS
( "QuickList.Release";
$list
)
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "QuickList.New"; "Hello¶World¶Test" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "QuickList.Release"; $list)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: QuickList.Remove
functions.description: Removes the value with the given index.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the item to remove. Range from 0 to QuickList.Count -1.

functions.name: QuickList.RemoveDuplicateItems
functions.description: Removes duplicate items in the list. Similar to UniqueValues function in FileMaker to find unique values.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: RemoveAll
parameters.description: If duplicate is found also remove the first occurrence. Set to 1 to enable or 0 to disable. Default is disabled.

parameters.name: ReturnNewList
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. This new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to modify the list in the List1 parameter.

parameters.name: CaseInsensitive
parameters.description: Whether to perform comparison case insensitve. By default we do case sensitive operations, which are faster. Pass 1 for insensitve or 0 for sensitive.

functions.name: QuickList.RemoveEmptyItems
functions.description: Removes empty items in the list.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

functions.name: QuickList.RemovePostfix
functions.description: Removes a postfix text to each entry in the list.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: Postfix
parameters.description: The text to remove.

parameters.name: ReturnNewList
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. This new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to modify the list in the List1 parameter.

parameters.name: CaseSensitive
parameters.description: Whether to match with case sensitive comparison. Default is 0 for case insensitive. You can pass 1 to have a case sensitive comparison.

examples.label: Try in one let statement:
examples.text_code:
Let([
list =
MBS
("
QuickList.New
"; ">Hello<¶>World<");
e =
MBS
("
QuickList.RemovePrefix
"; list; ">");
e =
MBS
("QuickList.RemovePostfix"; list; "<");
r =
MBS
("
QuickList.GetList
"; list);
d =
MBS
("
QuickList.Release
"; list)
]; r)
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
list = MBS("QuickList.New"; ">Hello<¶>World<");
e = MBS("QuickList.RemovePrefix"; list; ">");
e = MBS("QuickList.RemovePostfix"; list; "<");
r = MBS("QuickList.GetList"; list);
d = MBS("QuickList.Release"; list)
]; r)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: QuickList.RemovePrefix
functions.description: Removes a prefix text to each entry in the list.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: Prefix
parameters.description: The text to remove.

parameters.name: ReturnNewList
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. This new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to modify the list in the List1 parameter.

parameters.name: CaseSensitive
parameters.description: Whether to match with case sensitive comparison. Default is 0 for case insensitive. You can pass 1 to have a case sensitive comparison.

examples.label: Try in one let statement:
examples.text_code:
Let([
list =
MBS
("
QuickList.New
"; ">Hello<¶>World<");
e =
MBS
("QuickList.RemovePrefix"; list; ">");
e =
MBS
("
QuickList.RemovePostfix
"; list; "<");
r =
MBS
("
QuickList.GetList
"; list);
d =
MBS
("
QuickList.Release
"; list)
]; r)
examples.xml_code:

list = MBS("QuickList.New"; ">Hello<¶>World<");
e = MBS("QuickList.RemovePrefix"; list; ">");
e = MBS("QuickList.RemovePostfix"; list; "<");
r = MBS("QuickList.GetList"; list);
d = MBS("QuickList.Release"; list)
]; r)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: QuickList.RemoveValue
functions.description: Removes a value from a quick list.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to remove.

parameters.name: All
parameters.description: Optional, pass 1 to remove all values with the given value. Pass 0 (default) to remove only the first matching value.

functions.name: QuickList.RemoveValues
functions.description: We remove all values from the list. For each value in the values list, we find it in the first list and remove it if found.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: ValuesRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

functions.name: QuickList.Reserve
functions.description: Reserves memory for a list of given size. To ensure upfront that enough memory is available. This will not change the count, just preallocate memory.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: The new size in entries. So passing 100 will make sure we have space for 100 list entries at least. If more are added, the list grows automatically.

functions.name: QuickList.Reverse
functions.description: Reverses the order of items in the list.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

functions.name: QuickList.Serialize
functions.description: Serialized a list as a text. The dictionary content is stored in a base64 encoded string which the plugin function List.Deserialize and QuickList.Deserialize can read. Using this function allows to store a list in a text field or transmit it with Socket functions, SerialPort functions or by writing it to a file.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

examples.label: Create and save a list to restore it:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.New
"; "Hello¶World¶1234¶Just a test!")]
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:
MBS
("Quicklist.Serialize";
$list
)]
Set Variable [
$SecondList
; Value:
MBS
("
QuickList.Deserialize
";
$text
)]
Show Custom Dialog ["List"; "Encoded: " &
$text
& ¶ & ¶ & "Decoded: " &
MBS
("
QuickList.GetList
";
$secondList
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
QuickList.Release
";
$list
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
QuickList.Release
";
$secondList
)]

    </Repetition>

functions.name: QuickList.SetList
functions.description: Sets the list to the content of the passed list.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: New content for the list.

parameters.name: Separator
parameters.description: The separator to use. Can be ASCII character number (e.g. 9 for tab) or a text where we use first character as separator.

functions.name: QuickList.SetValue
functions.description: Sets a value in the list. If index is equal to count, we append the text to the list.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the value to get. Must be in range from 0 to QuickList.Count -1.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to add.

functions.name: QuickList.Sort
functions.description: Sorts the list. Version 6.2 adds new modes for date sorting. Mode 4 for DD.MM.YYYY style and mode 8 for MM.DD.YYYY style. Add mode 16 for flags to sort by numbers for version 11.0.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Optional, whether to sort case insensitive or descending. Pass 1 to sort case insensitive or 0 to sort case sensitive. Add 2 to sort descending. Add 4 for sorting dates in DD.MM.YYYY style and 8 for sorting dates in MM.DD.YYYY style.

examples.label: Sort a list:
examples.text_code:
Let
([
l =
MBS
("
QuickList.New
"; "a¶e¶ä¶f¶1");
r =
MBS
("QuickList.Sort"; l);
list =
MBS
("
QuickList.GetList
"; l);
r =
MBS
("
QuickList.Release
"; l)
]; list )
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let 
([
l = MBS("QuickList.New"; "a¶e¶ä¶f¶1");
r = MBS("QuickList.Sort"; l);
list = MBS("QuickList.GetList"; l);
r = MBS("QuickList.Release"; l)
]; list )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: QuickList.SortWith
functions.description: Sorts the list with together with other lists. We sort the first list and move all items in other lists as well to match the order. You can pass as many lists in the parameters as needed, not just 2. Add mode 16 for flags to sort by numbers for version 11.0. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat List2 parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Optional, whether to sort case insensitive or descending. Pass 1 to sort case insensitive or 0 to sort case sensitive. Add 2 to sort descending. Add 4 for sorting dates in DD.MM.YYYY style and 8 for sorting dates in MM.DD.YYYY style. Add 16 to sort by numbers.

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The first list to sort with.

parameters.name: List2...
parameters.description: The second list to sort with.

examples.label: Test sorting with four lists:
examples.text_code:
# build some lists
Set Variable [
$keys
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.New
"; "234¶123¶545¶152" )]
Set Variable [
$firstnames
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.New
"; "Bob¶John¶Bill¶Chris" )]
Set Variable [
$lastnames
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.New
"; "Miller¶Jones¶Becks¶Meyer" )]
Set Variable [
$cities
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.New
"; "New York¶Santa Clara¶San Francisco¶Atlanta" )]
# show before
Set Variable [
$r1
; Value:
MBS
( "
List.CrossProduct
";
MBS
("
QuickList.GetList
";
$keys
);
MBS
("
QuickList.GetList
";
$firstnames
); ""; ": "; ""; 2)]
Set Variable [
$r2
; Value:
MBS
( "
List.CrossProduct
";
MBS
("
QuickList.GetList
";
$lastnames
);
MBS
("
QuickList.GetList
";
$cities
); ""; ", "; ""; 2)]
Set Variable [
$r3
; Value:
MBS
( "
List.CrossProduct
";
$r1
;
$r2
; ""; ", "; ""; 2)]
# sort
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "QuickList.SortWith";
$keys
; 0;
$firstnames
;
$lastnames
;
$cities
)]
# show result:
Set Variable [
$r1
; Value:
MBS
( "
List.CrossProduct
";
MBS
("
QuickList.GetList
";
$keys
);
MBS
("
QuickList.GetList
";
$firstnames
); ""; ": "; ""; 2)]
Set Variable [
$r2
; Value:
MBS
( "
List.CrossProduct
";
MBS
("
QuickList.GetList
";
$lastnames
);
MBS
("
QuickList.GetList
";
$cities
); ""; ", "; ""; 2)]
Set Variable [
$r4
; Value:
MBS
( "
List.CrossProduct
";
$r1
;
$r2
; ""; ", "; ""; 2)]
Show Custom Dialog ["Sort results"; "Before: " & ¶ &
$r3
& ¶ &¶ & "Sorted: " & ¶ &
$r4
]
# cleanup memory
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.Release
";
$keys
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.Release
";
$cities
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.Release
";
$firstnames
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
QuickList.Release
";
$lastnames
)]

functions.name: QuickList.SortWithEvaluate
functions.description: Sorts a list with using an expression to evaluate. The expression is run every time two values are compared, so keep it quick. It should return 1 if leftValue is smaller than rightValue. Our normal QuickList.Sort is faster due to not having the overhead of evaluating expressions.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can include "leftValue" and "rightValue" as variables.

functions.name: QuickList.SubList
functions.signature: MBS( "QuickList.SubList"; ListRef; Index; Count { ; ReturnNewList; NoReturnEnding } )
functions.description: Returns a part of the list. Can return new QuickList or normal list.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The zero based index where to start. Or in other words: How many entries to skip.

parameters.name: Count
parameters.description: How many entries to copy. If negative, we pick the other items left.

parameters.name: ReturnNewList
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. This new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to return a text list.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: If you passed 0 for ReturnNewList, decides about new list: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

functions.name: QuickList.Trim
functions.description: Trims all texts in the list.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

functions.name: QuickList.ValueIndex
functions.description: Finds the index for a given value. Returns zero based index (0 = first list value). Returns empty result if not found. Comparison is case insensitive.

parameters.name: ListRef
parameters.description: The reference to the list returned from QuickList.New function.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The text to find.

functions.name: QuickList.XOr
functions.description: Returns list with values found in either first or second list, but not in both. Performs an exclusive logical OR operation.

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The reference to the first quicklist.

parameters.name: List2
parameters.description: The reference to the second quicklist.

parameters.name: ReturnNewList
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. This new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to modify the list in the List1 parameter.

functions.name: QuickList.XOrColumn
functions.signature: MBS( "QuickList.XOrColumn"; List1; List2; Separator; Column { ; ReturnNewList } )
functions.description: Returns list with values found in either first or second list, but not in both. Performs an exclusive logical OR operation. Checks the given column in both lists too compare. If Separator is empty, this is same as QuickList.XOr , but slower.

parameters.name: List1
parameters.description: The reference to the first quicklist.

parameters.name: List2
parameters.description: The reference to the second quicklist.

parameters.name: Separator
parameters.description: The separator text. Can be multiple characters. For tab separated text, pass Char(9). For CSV separated text, pass ";" or ",".

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The index of the column to compare. Starts with 0 for first column.

parameters.name: ReturnNewList
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return result as new QuickList. This new list must be freed later using QuickList.Release function. Pass 0 (Default) to modify the list in the List1 parameter.

functions.name: Register
functions.signature: MBS( "Register"; Licensee Name; Component; License Type; Expire Month; Serial number )
functions.description: This function is used to register the Plugin. If you use Server and Client licenses, be sure to register the right license for the right type. e.g. in your script detect if you are on the server or not. Before you register, you may want to check if already is registered. See also StoreRegistration , IsRegistration, IsRuntime , IsServer and IsClient functions. Newer plugins since 8.4 call StoreRegistration for iOS and Server automatically, so plugin registers automatically on loading and avoid problems with process is relaunched. You can later check status with Plugin.State , Plugin.IsLicenseKeyHidden or Plugin.IsMaintenanceExpired function and get licensee name with Plugin.LicenseeName function or seats count with Plugin.Seats function.

parameters.name: Licensee Name
parameters.description: This is the name of the person or organization that holds the license for the plug-in

parameters.name: Component
parameters.description: The component that was licensed. For example "Complete"

parameters.name: License Type
parameters.description: Seats purchased. For example, "1 seat", "10 seats", "Developer"

parameters.name: Expire Month
parameters.description: This is the month in which the serial will expire for new versions of the plug-in in YYYYMM format. Plug-in released before the end of that month will continue to work forever. New Releases after that date will require a renewal

parameters.name: Serial number
parameters.description: The license number

MBS

If [
MBS
("IsRuntime")]

$r
; Value:

Else If [
MBS
("IsMobile")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Register"; "test"; "iOS"; 123; 123)]
Else If [
MBS
("IsServer")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Register"; "test"; "Server"; 123; 123)]
Else
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Register"; "test"; "5 seats"; 123; 123)]
End If
End If

	End If
End If</Calculation>

    </Repetition>

examples.label: Register with Startup script for server and client:
examples.text_code:
# in Startup Script:
#Register for client
Perform Script [“InitMBS”]
#Register for server. Will be ignored if no server or no plugin installed on server
Perform Script on Server [“InitMBS”]
#InitMBS script:
#Enable debug logging. Shows messages in DebugView/Console.app
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Trace")]
#Register if needed for right platform
If [
MBS
("IsRegistered") = 0]
If [
MBS
("IsRuntime")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Register"; "test"; "Complete"; "Runtime"; 123; 123)]
Else If [
MBS
("IsServer")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Register"; "test"; "Complete"; "Server"; 123; 123)]
Else If [
MBS
("IsClient")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Register"; "test"; "Complete"; "5 Seats"; 123; 123)]
Else
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:"Unknown platform: " &
MBS
("Platform")]
End If
#Show errors, so developer can fix them
If [
$r
≠ "OK"]
Show Custom Dialog ["InitMBS failed.";
$r
]
End If
End If
#May initialize other stuff
#like dynapdf with library in extensions folder
If [
MBS
("
DynaPDF.IsInitialized
") = 0]
Set Variable [
$DynaPDFLicense
; Value:"1003637-16022016-3-8-12-685C57F..."]
If [
MBS
("IsWindows")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
DynaPDF.Initialize
"; "dynapdf.dll";
$DynaPDFLicense
)]
Else If [
MBS
("IsMacOSX")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
DynaPDF.Initialize
"; "dynapdf.dylib";
$DynaPDFLicense
)]
Else
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:"Unknown platform: " &
MBS
("Platform")]
End If
#Show errors, so developer can fix them
If [
$r
≠ "OK"]
Show Custom Dialog ["InitMBS failed.";
$r
]
End If
End If

functions.name: RSA.DecryptPrivateKey
functions.signature: MBS( "RSA.DecryptPrivateKey"; KeyType; KeyData; KeyEncoding; PasswordType; PasswordData { ; PasswordEncoding } )
functions.description: Decrypts a private key. Returns error if key could not be read, e.g. due to invalid password.

parameters.name: KeyType
parameters.description: The type of input for private key. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: KeyData
parameters.description: The actual input data for the key. Text, container or file path. Must not be longer than the maximum key length of the given algorithm. If key is too short, we pad it with zero bytes.

parameters.name: KeyEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input for the key. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: PasswordType
parameters.description: The type of input for optional password. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: PasswordData
parameters.description: The actual input data for the password. Text, container or file path.

parameters.name: PasswordEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

functions.result: Returns key as text or error.

examples.label: Encrypt private key and decrypt it:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$key1
; Value:
MBS
( "
RSA.GeneratePrivateKey
") ]
Set Variable [
$key2
; Value:
MBS
( "
RSA.EncryptPrivateKey
"; "Text";
$key1
; "UTF-8"; "AES-128-CFB"; "text"; "Hello") ]
Set Variable [
$key3
; Value:
MBS
( "RSA.DecryptPrivateKey"; "Text";
$key1
; "UTF-8"; "text"; "Hello") ]
examples.xml_code:

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$key1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

      <Calculation>MBS( "RSA.EncryptPrivateKey"; "Text"; $key1; "UTF-8"; "AES-128-CFB"; "text"; "Hello") </Calculation>
    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$key2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$key3</Name>

functions.name: RSA.EncryptPrivateKey
functions.signature: MBS( "RSA.EncryptPrivateKey"; KeyType; KeyData; KeyEncoding; Algorithm; PasswordType; PasswordData { ; PasswordEncoding } )
functions.description: Encrypts a private key. Returns error if key could not be read, e.g. because it is already encrypted.

parameters.name: KeyType
parameters.description: The type of input for private key. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: KeyData
parameters.description: The actual input data for the key. Text, container or file path. Must not be longer than the maximum key length of the given algorithm. If key is too short, we pad it with zero bytes.

parameters.name: KeyEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input for the key. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: Algorithm
parameters.description: Which algorithm to use. Can be for example RC2, blowfish, AES128, AES192, AES256. See Encryption.Cipher .

parameters.name: PasswordType
parameters.description: The type of input for optional password. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: PasswordData
parameters.description: The actual input data for the password. Text, container or file path.

parameters.name: PasswordEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

functions.result: Returns key as text or error.

examples.label: Encrypt private key and decrypt it:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$key1
; Value:
MBS
( "
RSA.GeneratePrivateKey
") ]
Set Variable [
$key2
; Value:
MBS
( "RSA.EncryptPrivateKey"; "Text";
$key1
; "UTF-8"; "AES-128-CFB"; "text"; "Hello") ]
Set Variable [
$key3
; Value:
MBS
( "
RSA.DecryptPrivateKey
"; "Text";
$key1
; "UTF-8"; "text"; "Hello") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "RSA.GeneratePrivateKey") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$key1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "RSA.EncryptPrivateKey"; "Text"; $key1; "UTF-8"; "AES-128-CFB"; "text"; "Hello") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$key2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "RSA.DecryptPrivateKey"; "Text"; $key1; "UTF-8"; "text"; "Hello") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$key3</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: RSA.GeneratePrivateKey
functions.signature: MBS( "RSA.GeneratePrivateKey" { ; Bits; Exp; Algorithm; PasswordType; PasswordData; PasswordEncoding } )
functions.description: Creates a new private key. Returns the PEM formatted key as text. As the PEM format contains base64 encoded DER data, you can remove header and footer line and use the result as base64 encoded DER key. If you specify an algorithm and a key, we encrypt the PEM file. (New in 7.5)

parameters.name: Bits
parameters.description: The bit size. e.g. 1024, 2048, 4096, etc. Default is 4096.

parameters.name: Exp
parameters.description: The exponent to use. Default is 65537.

parameters.name: Algorithm
parameters.description: Which algorithm to use. Can be for example RC2, blowfish, AES128, AES192, AES256. See Encryption.Cipher .

parameters.name: PasswordType
parameters.description: The type of input for optional password. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: PasswordData
parameters.description: The actual input data for the password. Text, container or file path.

parameters.name: PasswordEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

functions.result: Returns key as text or error.

examples.label: Generate keys:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$privateKey
; Value:
MBS
( "RSA.GeneratePrivateKey")]
Set Variable [
$publickey
; Value:
MBS
( "
RSA.GetPublicKey
"; "text";
$privateKey
; "UTF-8")]
Set Field [
Table::PrivateKey
;
$privateKey
]
Set Field [
Table::PublicKey
;
$publicKey
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "RSA.GeneratePrivateKey")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$privateKey</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "RSA.GetPublicKey"; "text"; $privateKey; "UTF-8")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$publickey</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$privateKey</Calculation>
    <Field table="Table" id="99999" name="PrivateKey"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$publicKey</Calculation>
    <Field table="Table" id="99999" name="PublicKey"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Generate key with text password:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "RSA.GeneratePrivateKey"; 4096; 65537; "AES-128-CBC"; "text"; "Hello" )

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "RSA.GeneratePrivateKey"; 4096; 65537; "AES-128-CBC"; "text"; "Hello" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: RSA.GetPublicKey
functions.signature: MBS( "RSA.GetPublicKey"; KeyType; KeyData { ; KeyEncoding; PasswordType; PasswordData; PasswordEncoding } )
functions.description: Queries public key from private key. You pass in private key and this function returns public key as PEM. Returns public key in PEM format as text. You can create a new private key using RSA.GeneratePrivateKey function.

parameters.name: KeyType
parameters.description: The type of input for private key. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: KeyData
parameters.description: The actual input data for the key. Text, container or file path. Must not be longer than the maximum key length of the given algorithm. If key is too short, we pad it with zero bytes.

parameters.name: KeyEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input for the key. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: PasswordType
parameters.description: The type of input for the password of the private key. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: PasswordData
parameters.description: The actual input data for the password of the private key. Text, container or file path. Must not be longer than the maximum key length of the given algorithm. If key is too short, we pad it with zero bytes.

parameters.name: PasswordEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

functions.name: RSA.PrivateDecrypt
functions.signature: MBS( "RSA.PrivateDecrypt"; InputType; InputData; InputEncoding; KeyType; KeyData; KeyEncoding; OutputType { ; OutputInfo; PasswordType; PasswordData; PasswordEncoding; Padding } )
functions.description: Decrypts data using private key. Using RSA keys with any key size. Password is optional for decrypting encrypted keys. You can decrypt the data here after encrypting it with RSA.PublicEncrypt . You can create a new private key using RSA.GeneratePrivateKey function.

parameters.name: InputType
parameters.description: The type of input data. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: InputData
parameters.description: The actual input data. Text, container or file path.

parameters.name: InputEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: KeyType
parameters.description: The type of input for private key. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: KeyData
parameters.description: The actual input data for the key. Text, container or file path. Must not be longer than the maximum key length of the given algorithm. If key is too short, we pad it with zero bytes.

parameters.name: KeyEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input for the key. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: OutputType
parameters.description: The output type. Can be Base64, Hex, Text, File, Path.

parameters.name: OutputInfo
parameters.description: The addition info for output. For text it is the text encoding, for file container the file name and for path the actual native file path.

parameters.name: PasswordType
parameters.description: The type of input for the password of the private key. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: PasswordData
parameters.description: The actual input data for the password of the private key. Text, container or file path. Must not be longer than the maximum key length of the given algorithm. If key is too short, we pad it with zero bytes.

parameters.name: PasswordEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: Padding
parameters.description: Which padding to use. 1: PKCS #1 v1.5 padding. This currently is the most widely used mode. 2: PKCS #1 v1.5 padding with an SSL-specific modification that denotes that the server is SSL3 capable. 3: Raw RSA encryption. This mode should only be used to implement cryptographically sound padding modes in the application code. Encrypting user data directly with RSA is insecure. 4: EME-OAEP as defined in PKCS #1 v2.0 with SHA-1, MGF1 and an empty encoding parameter. This mode is recommended for all new applications. 5: X931 padding. Default if not specified is 1.

examples.label: Decrypt with private key:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "RSA.PrivateDecrypt"; "base64";
RSA Public Private Encryption::EncryptedText
; ""; "Text";
RSA Public Private Encryption::PrivateKey
; "UTF-8"; "Text"; "UTF-8")]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Error";
$r
]
Else
Set Field [
RSA Public Private Encryption::DecryptedText
;
$r
]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "RSA.PrivateDecrypt"; "base64"; RSA Public Private Encryption::EncryptedText; ""; "Text"; RSA Public Private Encryption::PrivateKey; "UTF-8"; "Text"; "UTF-8")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("IsError")</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Error"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$r</Calculation>
    <Field table="RSA Public Private Encryption" id="99999" name="DecryptedText"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: RSA.PublicDecrypt
functions.signature: MBS( "RSA.PublicDecrypt"; InputType; InputData; InputEncoding; KeyType; KeyData; KeyEncoding; OutputType { ; OutputInfo; PasswordType; PasswordData; PasswordEncoding; Padding } )
functions.description: Decrypts data using public key. Using RSA keys with any key size. Password is optional for decrypting encrypted keys. You can decrypt the data here after encrypting it with RSA.PrivateEncrypt . You can create a new private key using RSA.GeneratePrivateKey function. The RSA.GetPublicKey function extracts the public key from a private key. Because of the nature of the RSA algorithm, a single encryption process can only encrypt input data that is smaller than the modulus value of the RSA key. In other words, the size (number of bytes) of the input data should be smaller than the size (number bytes) of the modulus, which is also the RSA key size.

parameters.name: InputType
parameters.description: The type of input data. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: InputData
parameters.description: The actual input data. Text, container or file path.

parameters.name: InputEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: KeyType
parameters.description: The type of input for private key. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: KeyData
parameters.description: The actual input data for the key. Text, container or file path. Must not be longer than the maximum key length of the given algorithm. If key is too short, we pad it with zero bytes.

parameters.name: KeyEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input for the key. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: OutputType
parameters.description: The output type. Can be Base64, Hex, Text, File, Path.

parameters.name: OutputInfo
parameters.description: The addition info for output. For text it is the text encoding, for file container the file name and for path the actual native file path.

parameters.name: PasswordType
parameters.description: The type of input for the password of the private key. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: PasswordData
parameters.description: The actual input data for the password of the private key. Text, container or file path. Must not be longer than the maximum key length of the given algorithm. If key is too short, we pad it with zero bytes.

parameters.name: PasswordEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: Padding
parameters.description: Which padding to use. 1: PKCS #1 v1.5 padding. This currently is the most widely used mode. 2: PKCS #1 v1.5 padding with an SSL-specific modification that denotes that the server is SSL3 capable. 3: Raw RSA encryption. This mode should only be used to implement cryptographically sound padding modes in the application code. Encrypting user data directly with RSA is insecure. 4: EME-OAEP as defined in PKCS #1 v2.0 with SHA-1, MGF1 and an empty encoding parameter. This mode is recommended for all new applications. 5: X931 padding. Default if not specified is 1.

functions.name: RSA.Sign
functions.signature: MBS( "RSA.Sign"; Algorithm; InputType; InputData; InputEncoding; KeyType; KeyData; KeyEncoding; PasswordType; PasswordData; PasswordEncoding; OutputType { ; OutputInfo } )
functions.description: Signs data with RSA digital signature. Returns signature data in the format specified in OutputType. Internally we make a hash of the data with given algorithm, open the private RSA key and do a RSA sign operation. We return the signature. Optional you can pass a password to read password protected keys. You pass data as triplet of type, data and encoding. For the types use: Text: Pass text and encoding. Data or File: Pass a container to read its content as data. PDF: Pass a container containing a PDF document. Container: Pass a container, which will be serialized. Image: Pass a container containing an image, e.g. JPEG, TIFF, PNG, GIF, etc. GIF: Pass a container containing a GIF document. JPEG: Pass a container containing a JPEG document. PNG: Pass a container containing a PNG document. TIFF: Pass a container containing a TIFF document. Path: Pass a file name as data. The file is read and it's content is used. Hex: Pass a hex encoded value as text. Hash: Pass the hex encoded hash to use instead of hashing data. How to create keys and test in command line on macOS or Linux: Create private key: openssl genrsa -out test.pem 4096 Create public key: openssl rsa -in test.pem -pubout -out test.pub Sign datafile.txt: openssl dgst -sha1 -sign test.pem -out signature.sha1 Create\ Keys.rtf Verify signature openssl dgst -sha1 -verify test.pub -signature signature.sha1 Create\ Keys.rtf To encrypt the private key, use a command like this: openssl genrsa -des3 -out test.pem 2048

parameters.name: Algorithm
parameters.description: Which algorithm to use. Can be MD4, MD5, SHA, SHA1, MDC2, RIPEMD160, SHA224, SHA256, SHA384 or SHA512.

parameters.name: InputType
parameters.description: The type of input data. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: InputData
parameters.description: The actual input data. Text, container or file path.

parameters.name: InputEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: KeyType
parameters.description: The type of input for private key. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: KeyData
parameters.description: The actual input data for the key. Text, container or file path. Must not be longer than the maximum key length of the given algorithm. If key is too short, we pad it with zero bytes.

parameters.name: KeyEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input for the key. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: PasswordType
parameters.description: The type of input for the password of the private key. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: PasswordData
parameters.description: The actual input data for the password of the private key. Text, container or file path. Must not be longer than the maximum key length of the given algorithm. If key is too short, we pad it with zero bytes.

parameters.name: PasswordEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: OutputType
parameters.description: The output type. Can be Base64, Hex, Text, File, Path. Added Base64URL as option for plugin version 10.3.

parameters.name: OutputInfo
parameters.description: The addition info for output. For text it is the text encoding, for file container the file name and for path the actual native file path.

"UTF8";

</fmxmlsnippet>

    <Calculation>MBS( "RSA.Sign"; Table::Algorithm; "Text"; Table::Text to Sign; "UTF8"; "text"; Table::PrivateKey; "UTF8"; ""; ""; ""; "hex"; "" )</Calculation>

examples.label: Calculate JWT with RS256:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
secret = "secretkey";
header = JWT RS256::Header;
payload = JWT RS256::Payload;
encodedString = MBS( "Text.EncodeToBase64URL"; header; "UTF-8" ) & "." & MBS( "Text.EncodeToBase64URL"; payload; "UTF-8" );

// calculate hash
hash = MBS( "RSA.Sign"; "SHA256"; 
"Text"; encodedString; "UTF-8"; 
"Text"; JWT RS256::Key; "UTF-8"; 
"text"; JWT RS256::Password; "UTF-8"; 
"base64url"; "");

// and built final result:
result = encodedString & "." & hash
]; result )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: RSA.SignPSS
functions.signature: MBS( "RSA.SignPSS"; Algorithm; InputType; InputData; InputEncoding; KeyType; KeyData; KeyEncoding; PasswordType; PasswordData; PasswordEncoding; OutputType { ; OutputInfo; SaltLength } )
functions.description: Signs data with RSA digital signature with PSS padding. Returns signature data in the format specified in OutputType. See also RSA.Sign for more details. Special values for salt length:

parameters.name: Algorithm
parameters.description: Which algorithm to use. Can be MD4, MD5, SHA, SHA1, MDC2, RIPEMD160, SHA224, SHA256, SHA384 or SHA512.

parameters.name: InputType
parameters.description: The type of input data. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: InputData
parameters.description: The actual input data. Text, container or file path.

parameters.name: InputEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: KeyType
parameters.description: The type of input for private key. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: KeyData
parameters.description: The actual input data for the key. Text, container or file path. Must not be longer than the maximum key length of the given algorithm. If key is too short, we pad it with zero bytes.

parameters.name: KeyEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input for the key. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: PasswordType
parameters.description: The type of input for the password of the private key. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: PasswordData
parameters.description: The actual input data for the password of the private key. Text, container or file path. Must not be longer than the maximum key length of the given algorithm. If key is too short, we pad it with zero bytes.

parameters.name: PasswordEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: OutputType
parameters.description: The output type. Can be Base64, Hex, Text, File, Path. Added Base64URL as option for plugin version 10.3.

parameters.name: OutputInfo
parameters.description: The addition info for output. For text it is the text encoding, for file container the file name and for path the actual native file path.

parameters.name: SaltLength
parameters.description: The desired salt length in bytes. Default is -1.

functions.name: RSA.Verify
functions.signature: MBS( "RSA.Verify"; Algorithm; InputType; InputData; InputEncoding; KeyType; KeyData; KeyEncoding; SigType; SigData; SigEncoding { ; PasswordType; PasswordData; PasswordEncoding } )
functions.description: Verifies RSA digital signature. Returns 1 if signature is valid or 0 if not or an error if parameters are wrong. Key can be the public or private key, but of course normally you use the public key. A hash is created with given algorithm and verified with the signature. Signature must be the string returned like from RSA.Sign function. Optional you can pass a password to read password protected keys.

parameters.name: Algorithm
parameters.description: Which algorithm to use. Can be MD4, MD5, SHA, SHA1, MDC2, RIPEMD160, SHA224, SHA256, SHA384 or SHA512.

parameters.name: InputType
parameters.description: The type of input data. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: InputData
parameters.description: The actual input data. Text, container or file path.

parameters.name: InputEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: KeyType
parameters.description: The type of input for private or public key. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: KeyData
parameters.description: The actual input data for the key. Text, container or file path. Must not be longer than the maximum key length of the given algorithm. If key is too short, we pad it with zero bytes.

parameters.name: KeyEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input for the key. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: SigType
parameters.description: The type of input data for signature. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: SigData
parameters.description: The actual input data for signature. Text, container or file path.

parameters.name: SigEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: PasswordType
parameters.description: The type of input for the password of the private key. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: PasswordData
parameters.description: The actual input data for the password of the private key. Text, container or file path. Must not be longer than the maximum key length of the given algorithm. If key is too short, we pad it with zero bytes.

parameters.name: PasswordEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

"SHA1"; 
"Path"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/Sign and Verify/Create Keys.rtf"; "";

"Path"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/Sign and Verify/signature.sha1"; ""; 
"Text"; ""; "" )</Calculation>

"Text"; ""; "" )</Calculation>

examples.label: Verify some text:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "RSA.Verify";
Table::Algorithm
;  "Text";
Table::Text
to Sign; "UTF8";  "Text";
Table::PublicKey
; "UTF8";  "Hex";
Table::Signature
)]
If [
$r
= 1]
Show Custom Dialog ["Signature valid"]
Else If [
$r
= 0]
Show Custom Dialog ["Signature invalid"]
Else
Show Custom Dialog ["Error";
MBS
("
Text.RemovePrefix
";
$r
; "[
MBS
] ")]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "RSA.Verify";  Table::Algorithm;  "Text"; Table::Text to Sign; "UTF8";  "Text"; Table::PublicKey; "UTF8";  "Hex"; Table::Signature )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$r = 1</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Signature valid"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="125" name="Else If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$r = 0</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Signature invalid"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Error"; MBS("Text.RemovePrefix"; $r</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"[MBS] ")</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: RSA.VerifyPSS
functions.signature: MBS( "RSA.VerifyPSS"; Algorithm; InputType; InputData; InputEncoding; KeyType; KeyData; KeyEncoding; SigType; SigData; SigEncoding { ; PasswordType; PasswordData; PasswordEncoding; SaltLength } )
functions.description: Verifies RSA digital signature with PSS padding. Returns 1 if signature is valid or 0 if not or an error if parameters are wrong. See also RSA.Verify for more details. Special values for salt length:

parameters.name: Algorithm
parameters.description: Which algorithm to use. Can be MD4, MD5, SHA, SHA1, MDC2, RIPEMD160, SHA224, SHA256, SHA384 or SHA512.

parameters.name: InputType
parameters.description: The type of input data. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: InputData
parameters.description: The actual input data. Text, container or file path.

parameters.name: InputEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: KeyType
parameters.description: The type of input for private or public key. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: KeyData
parameters.description: The actual input data for the key. Text, container or file path. Must not be longer than the maximum key length of the given algorithm. If key is too short, we pad it with zero bytes.

parameters.name: KeyEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input for the key. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: SigType
parameters.description: The type of input data for signature. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: SigData
parameters.description: The actual input data for signature. Text, container or file path.

parameters.name: SigEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: PasswordType
parameters.description: The type of input for the password of the private key. Can be Text, Container, Image, Path, PDF, Data, base64 or Hex.

parameters.name: PasswordData
parameters.description: The actual input data for the password of the private key. Text, container or file path. Must not be longer than the maximum key length of the given algorithm. If key is too short, we pad it with zero bytes.

parameters.name: PasswordEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text input. Parameter is ignored for other cases.

parameters.name: SaltLength
parameters.description: The desired salt length in bytes. Default is -1.

parameters.name: Index

functions.name: SharingPanel.ItemCount

functions.name: SharingPanel.Present
functions.description: Presents the sharing panel. This will show a view controller that you use to offer standard services from your application. Your script may call this in a script and then exit. Wait for the script trigger, while no script runs.

examples.label: Share a picture from container:

" ) ]

";

) ]

( "
SharingPanel.SetScript
"; Get(FileName); "Dismissed" ) ]
# Show the window

; Value:

( "SharingPanel.Present" ) ]

functions.name: SharingPanel.SetScript
functions.description: Sets the script to call when sharing panel is done. In the script you can check with SharingPanel.Result , SharingPanel.ErrorText and SharingPanel.ErrorCode what happened. If the activity provides an answer, check SharingPanel.Item and SharingPanel.ItemCount functions to get them. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name for the script to trigger.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name of the script to launch.

parameters.name: Parameter
parameters.description: The parameter to pass to the script.

$r
; Value:

( "SharingPanel.SetScript"; Get(FileName); "Shared") ]

functions.name: SpeechRecognition.AuthorizationStatus
functions.description: Queries authorization status. Returns your app's current authorization to perform speech recognition. The user can reject your app's request to perform speech recognition, but your request can also be denied if speech recognition is not supported on the device. The app can also change your app's authorization status at any time from the Settings app.

parameters.name: NotDetermined
parameters.description: The app's authorization status has not yet been determined.

parameters.name: Denied
parameters.description: The user denied your app's request to perform speech recognition.

parameters.name: Restricted
parameters.description: The device prevents your app from performing speech recognition.

parameters.name: Authorized
parameters.description: The user granted your app's request to perform speech recognition.

functions.name: SpeechRecognition.Initialize
functions.description: Initializes the Speech Recognition. Returns OK on success for MacOS 10.15 and iOS 10 or newer.

functions.name: SpeechRecognition.IsInitialized
functions.description: Checks whether we are initialized. Returns 1 if initialized.

functions.name: SpeechRecognition.Recognize
functions.signature: MBS( "SpeechRecognition.Recognize"; FilePath { ; Locale; Hint; requiresOnDeviceRecognition; contextualStrings } )
functions.description: Recognizes some text. The plugin creates a new speech recognition request based on the given audio file. Then we let the system recognize the text and return it to you. This may take a while and the plugin returns after a few seconds when work is done. Requires MacOS 10.15 or iOS 10 or newer.

parameters.name: FilePath
parameters.description: The file path to the audio file to analyze.

parameters.name: Locale
parameters.description: The locale to use. Leave empty to use user's current locale.

parameters.name: Hint
parameters.description: The hint on what kind of speech recognition being performed Can be Search, Dictation, Confirmation or Unspecified. Default is Unspecified.

parameters.name: requiresOnDeviceRecognition
parameters.description: Whether you require on device recognition. Pass 1 to require on device recognition or 0 for the case it doesn't matter.

parameters.name: contextualStrings
parameters.description: Phrases which should be recognized even if they are not in the system vocabulary.

functions.name: SpeechRecognition.RequestAuthorization
functions.description: Requests authorization for Speech Recognition. Asks the user to allow your app to perform speech recognition. Call this method before performing any other tasks associated with speech recognition. This method executes asynchronously, returning shortly after you call it. This may show a dialog and user may confirm later. When your app's authorization status is NotDetermined, this method causes the system to prompt the user to grant or deny permission for your app to use speech recognition. The prompt includes the custom message you specify in the NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription key of your app's Info.plist file. The user's response is saved so that future calls to this method do not prompt the user again.

examples.label: Request authorization:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
;
MBS
( "SpeechRecognition.RequestAuthorization" ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SpeechRecognition.RequestAuthorization" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SpeechRecognition.SupportedLocales
functions.description: Queries supported locales. Returns the set of locales that are supported by the speech recognizer. This method returns the locales for which speech recognition is supported. Support for a locale does not guarantee that speech recognition is currently possible for that locale. For some locales, the speech recognizer requires an active Internet connection to communicate with Apple's servers. If the speech recognizer is currently unable to process requests, available returns false. Speech recognition supports the same locales that are supported by the keyboard's dictation feature.
functions.result: Returns list of locale identifiers or error.

examples.label: Query locales
examples.text_code:

; Value:

functions.name: SpeechRecognition.SupportsOnDeviceRecognition
functions.description: Queries whether on device recognition is supported. Returns 1 if supported or 0 if not.

parameters.name: Locale
parameters.description: The locale to use. Leave empty to use user's current locale.

examples.label: Check for on device recognition for US-English:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "SpeechRecognition.SupportsOnDeviceRecognition"; "en-US" )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SpeechRecognition.SupportsOnDeviceRecognition"; "en-US" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>
